name: Burger Me!
description:
PREMIUM LOCAL AND DELICIOUS!
Gourmet varities… Beef, Bison, Chicken, Turkey, Lamb, Ahi Tuna, Cod & Veggie Cravable & different…

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Casa Baeza Restaurant & Bar
description:
Indulge in the authentic flavors of Mexico at Casa Baeza in Truckee, California, where culinary tradition meets taste in every dish. With over 30 years of local legacy and an impressive 500+ 5-star Google reviews, Casa Baeza has become a beloved icon in the community.

Our diverse menu boasts expertly seasoned meats—Asada, beef, carnitas, pastor, chicken, shrimp, and fish—cooked with innovative and delicious culinary techniques and more to an endless selection of margaritas—you’re in for a real treat..
Each bite is a journey south of the border.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Cottonwood Restaurant & Bar
description:
At Cottonwood, we believe great meals should feel like home - warm, generous, and worth slowing down for. That’s why we cook with care, source with intention, and treat hospitality like a craft. 

Whether you’re a regular of just passing through, we set the table like you’re part of the family.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Craft & Logic Taproom
description:
Craft & Logic is a family-owned taproom & bottle shop offering an elevated craft beer experience in downtown Truckee, California.

We have over 30 taps pouring beer of many styles with a focus on self-distributed, highly allocated, craft-breweries from all over the country. Beers are constantly rotating and we are always on the lookout for new and exciting flavors.

In addition, there are rotating taps of hard-kombucha, cider, wine, and non-alcoholic options.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Donner Lake Inn
description: Nestled in the heart of Truckee and steps from Donner Lake, Donner Lake Inn offers a cozy escape for families, couples, and outdoor adventurers. Whether you’re here for the slopes, the trails, or a moment of quiet under the stars, your Sierra retreat begins here.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Drunken Monkey Sushi
description:
We love sushi, and we love Truckee, so it was natural for us to combine the two. At Drunken Monkey, we constantly innovate to bring new dishes to life. We push the boundaries of cuisine, with ingredients, techniques and flavor profiles so that your experience is unique whether you're a weeknight regular, or a first time visitor.

As innovation expands our access to great ingredients, we have developed relationships with the best suppliers on the West Coast. Our fish is brought in daily from the same suppliers serving some of San Francisco's most revered sushi restaurants. We work with local providers whenever we can to bring world-class fish together with local organic ingredients. The result is an experience that is respectful of the great traditions of sushi but definitively Truckee born.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Fifty Fifty Brewing Company
description: Laid-back local microbrewery & pub with a diverse menu plus retail beer & logo merchandise.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Full Belly Deli
description:
Full Belly Deli was born over a fresh-baked loaf of jalapeño-cheddar bread and two PBRs. Co-owners Eric Barton & Tom Marrin have a few things in common but the strongest bond is their love of a good deli sandwich.

Over a couple more fine brews, they laid the groundwork for a place the locals would love and embrace, a dining establishment tourists would seek out on any trip to the Truckee/Tahoe area, and the kind of spot that you couldn't wait to introduce to your friends and family.

In December of the 2007, the Town of Truckee changed forever when the boys simultaneously switched on the "Open" and "Pabst" neon signs at Full Belly Deli. Since Day One, the boys and their deli teamsters have been serving up the freshest ingredients combined in ways mere mortals could never even dream (Dorito-encrusted chicken? Biscuits & gravy burrito?) and they'll do it with an approach to customer service that is rare in the area.

Most regulars are on a first name basis with the crew but even if it's your first time there, you'll be treated like family.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Glenshire General Store
description:
Whether you’re joining us for lunch, picking up that one special ingredient, or just browsing our surprisingly great selection, everything you need is truly a stone’s throw from home.

Family owned and operated. We’re proud to feed our friends and neighbors!

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

description: Glenshire Pizza Company crafts delicious dishes with fresh, high-quality ingredients. Enjoy our pizzas, house-made pastas, chicken parmigiana, salads, burgers, and desserts.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

Located at 10144 W River St, Truckee, CA, we bring you the flavors of classic American diner fare in a welcoming, casual atmosphere. Whether you're here to start your day with a hearty breakfast, fuel up for lunch, or enjoy a satisfying dinner, we’ve got something to make everyone feel right at home. Non-Dairy Options as well! Organic Coconut Milk Based flavors and Organic fruit Sorbets offered every day.

The Truckee River and Truckee Legacy Trail are within five miles. Our seasonal outdoor pool and year-round hot tub, event room, and fitness center complete your stay.

description: Established in 2002, Moody’s Bistro is the quintessential Truckee experience. A warm, inviting space filled with locals, long-time visitors, and first-time tourists. A place for craft-cocktails, farm-to-table eats, and world-class, eclectic sounds. Where the staff is friendly and the music is always free. The recipes are often from the owners own family kitchen. We serve Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner with the convince of counter service  & dine in cozy vibe for a quicker dining experience in our mountain town.  We have a drive thru if you are not wanting to get out of the car and when the weather is right, you can ride your bike & dine outside.

We hope you enjoy our shop!

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

Every moment at Northstar fills you with a feeling unlike any other, one that persists generation after generation in this special place nestled in the Martis Valley.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

description:
Old Trestle began as a group of friends in Truckee, California who believed that the world's best whiskey had yet to be created. We take lessons from those that have come before us and look forward to create new traditions. Rooted in whiskey but today offering our Gins and Vodka, with more expressions to come as we grow.

As Truckee's first distillery in over 100 years, we are bringing the science and art of distilling back to the Sierra Nevada mountains.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Pianeta Ristorante
description: We are a cozy restaurant located in the heart of historic downtown Truckee, specializing in creating delicious Italian foods, homemade pastas, and stellar dining service since 1998. We feature an extensive menu with nightly specials inspired by the delicious cuisine of the Mediterranean. We also feature an impressive rotating wine list with numerous options from California and Italy, and a full bar featuring classic cocktails and aperitifs. We are defined by our welcoming friendly staff, remarkable consistent food, and warm eclectic old world ambiance.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Rubicon Pizza Company
description: Come enjoy our outstanding service, and quality menu offerings – all delivered in our warm and inviting atmosphere. We strive to exceed the guest's expectations for each dining experience. Our goal is to provide a memorable experience for all those that come in contact with our restaurant. Our greatest reward is hearing from our team about how our restaurants have touched their lives and how they love to come to work. Our food tastes great because we buy premium ingredients and strive to buy organic, locally sourced produce whenever possible. We specialize in Californian styled pizza dishes, but also brush the palette on a wide variety of pastas, salads, beers, wines, craft cocktails, and desserts.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Schaffer's Mill Golf & Lake Club
description:
Schaffer's Mill is a private mountain community where membership reflects a refined balance of comfort, connection, and natural beauty. Whether it's playing an incredible round of golf, skiing the powdered slopes of nearby Northstar, or spending time in Lake Tahoe, Schaffer's Mill is the perfect starting point for a lifetime of memories. Set between historic Truckee and stunning Lake Tahoe in one of the most desirable areas, Schaffer's Mill offers an exceptional four-season lifestyle surrounded by beautiful alpine scenery. It's a place where friends gather, where kids play, where neighbors become lifetime friends and where the word "community" means more than roads, homes and buildings.

Membership provides access to a warm and welcoming community united by shared experiences, genuine hospitality, and an appreciation for timeless alpine living. Whether spending the season or calling Schaffer's Mill home year-round, members enjoy an elevated standard of living in a place that feels both exclusive and inviting!

name: Sierra Bakehouse

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

description: Refresh at the SpringHill Suites Truckee. Our new pet-friendly Northern California hotel offers little extras that add up to an exceptional stay, with its crystal-clear lakes to its legendary skiing, hiking and golfing all a short drive away. Relax in our hotel suites with free Wi-Fi, plush Marriott bedding, and large workspaces. Start your day with selections from our free breakfast offering hot coffee, Belgian waffles, healthy yogurts, and more! You can also try local restaurants near our hotel like Truckee Food Shop, Trokay, and Squeeze In. Be your best self during travel with our fitness center and outdoor pool. During your stay enjoy easy access to attractions like Lake Tahoe, Historic Downtown Truckee, Donner Memorial State Park, and more. Plan your your upcoming meeting, wedding, or special occasion in our event space with special group rates when you book a block of suites for your guests. We look forward to welcoming you at the Springhill Suites Truckee whether you are here for business or pleasure!

Sierra sun best kid friendly restaurant winner 2023Voted best kid-friendly restaurant by Sierra Sun Readers, find out why families love Pizza on the Hill. With delicious pizza, classic appetizers and more the whole family can enjoy, plus a nearby playground and cozy outdoor seating, it’s no wonder Pizza on the Hill continues to be a fan favorite year after year. sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

name: Tahoe Getaways
description: With its deep blue lake surrounded by mountains and forests and endless outdoor activities in both summer and fall, Lake Tahoe vacation rentals are the best way to enjoy the most beautiful, year-round vacation destination in the country. Let Tahoe Getaways, with its extensive list of Tahoe rentals throughout North Tahoe and Truckee, help you find the perfect rental in Tahoe to enjoy this magical place and access all your favorite activities from your doorstep. Tahoe Getaways offers both great service and rental homes as extraordinary as their majestic setting. Use our Tahoe Guide as your handbook for learning which area to begin your search.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Tahoe Luxury Properties
description: Tahoe Luxury Properties is a full-service agency specializing in luxury vacation rentals and real estate sales on Lake Tahoe’s North & West Shores, Truckee, Olympic Valley, Alpine Meadows and Northstar in California; and Incline Village, Crystal Bay, and Tahoe's East Shore in Nevada. We are a team of experts living locally, available as a resource to you to help you understand the area and the market, whether you are looking to buy, sell, or vacation in Lake Tahoe & Truckee.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Tahoe Mountain Lodging
description: From comfortable to luxurious, East West Hospitality Tahoe offers private homes, cabins, villas, and condominiums with your choice of style, size, amenities, and location. Whether you want to stay in the bustling Village at Northstar, tucked away in the golf course community of Old Greenwood, or among the towering pines off Northstar Drive, these thoughtfully appointed vacation residences provide all the comforts of home. Enjoy family rooms, kitchens, beautiful views, garage parking, and indoor-outdoor living.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Tahoe Star Hotel
description:
Welcome to the Tahoe Star Hotel – the heart, soul, and history of Truckee and North Lake Tahoe area a mountain town community. Built in the 1870s by early settler and timberman George Schaffer, the hotel originally housed his family, then later it became lodging for his mill workers. Feel the spirit of the early emigrants, explorers, and lumberjacks with a stay at “Star Hotel ” Ideal for your small groups, solo travelers a romantic retreat or fun-filled family getaway and outdoor adventures.

Completely remodeled, the historic eight-room hotel features the modern conveniences you expect in fine lodging, excellent Wi-Fi to power your devices and clear cell phone receptivity, en-suite bathrooms with a shower, a historic charm and modern comforts, filtered water station, complimentary self-serve coffee, tea and snacks and a cozy shared space to unwind after a day of adventure.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

description: Tahoe Truckee Vacation Properties has been one of the leading property management companies in the Tahoe Truckee area since 2003, offering a seamless blend of local expertise and over 115 North Lake Tahoe rentals. Our website allows you to easily search and book vacation rentals in Truckee, Tahoe Donner, and North Lake Tahoe. Our local property management team ensures a personalized experience, with options ranging from pet-friendly rentals, to homes with hot tubs, and serene Truckee River homes. Dedicated to the highest service standards, we ensure all of our North Lake Tahoe properties are well-maintained for your arrival. With no third-party booking fees, Tahoe Truckee Vacation Properties promises a memorable vacation at a fair rate.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

description: Experience The Ritz-Carlton, Lake Tahoe, and discover the pristine beauty of the Sierra Nevada Mountain range. From its crystal-clear lake to its legendary skiing, hiking, and golfing – this luxury Lake Tahoe hotel provides endless possibilities for travelers of all ages and interests. Featuring the only luxury ski-in, ski-out access in Lake Tahoe with mountain concierge services, a stunning slopeside spa, exceptional dining, and impeccable service, The Ritz-Carlton, Lake Tahoe is the region's premier mountain retreat and lakefront hotel in California.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: The Truckee Hotel
description:
Located in the heart of historic downtown Truckee, when you walk into the lobby you will find a caring and gracious staff that will go out of their way to make you feel welcome and at home. The warm earth tone and rich wood complements the fresh mountain air as you ease into your stay with us. Every morning from 7-10 a.m. a continental breakfast of rich brewed coffee and herbal teas, baked goods, fruit, oatmeal/cereals and juice can be enjoyed in a warm setting while capturing the daily weather and news on a large flat screen TV.

The Truckee Hotel is centrally located to many North Lake Tahoe ski areas such as Squaw Valley, site of the 1960's Winter Olympics, as well as Northstar, Sugar Bowl, Alpine Meadows, Tahoe Donner, Royal Gorge and more, including Tahoe's latest action packed Woodward at Tahoe. While staying at the Truckee Hotel you can access all of North Lake Tahoe's best amenities, shop in Downtown Truckee or dine in the many restaurants and bistros nearby.

From our high-vaulted ceilings to our open-concept floor plan showcasing our state-of-the-art equipment, we invite you to come get comfortable and experience Truckee Brewing Company.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay

Our lodge combines modern luxuries and contemporary decor with the traditional amenities and architecture of a country lodge.  Whether you are on the ultimate ski vacation, traveling for business, or just need a weekend or weekday getaway, the Truckee Donner Lodge offers exceptional amenities at a price that is unexpected.

name: Uncorked Truckee
description: Uncorked Wine Bar and Retail Bottle Shop features a large selection of limited production wines from around the world. Focusing on producers that farm mindfully, that are the best representation for each given category, varietal and deliver for the price point. Petra is a full scale restaurant creating progressive, farm to table Mediterranean seasonal dishes, that are tailored to our wine program. We taste we read we learn we pour.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Village Camp Truckee
description: Village Camp Truckee-Tahoe is a perfect spot for those looking to escape into nature and find adventure. The cabins are eco-friendly, modern getaways that provide everything you need in one place. Adventure awaits outside the door with hiking trails and plenty of places to jump on your bike or head to Donner Lake just minutes away for water sport fun! You’ll never have trouble exploring because this area offers endless opportunities like visiting farmer’s markets around town or enjoying delicious food at farm-to-table restaurants nearby.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Wild Cherries Coffee House
description:
Wild Cherries Coffee House has been serving the community since 1998. Kristin Krone, a native Truckee resident and two time Olympian in alpine skiing, along with Gary Talbot, local entrepreneur and fellow coffee connoisseur became acquainted in 1984 at the first local coffee Roastery, Truckee River Coffee Roasters. A shared passion for excellent fresh roasted coffees, European style pastries and a place to meet friends eventually led to a business partnership.   In 1995 they began their own coffee roasting venture which ultimately led to Wild Cherries Coffee House in 1998. In 2003 Kristin became sole owner of Wild Cherries continuing the tradition she and Gary had created.
 
The inspiration and mission of Wild Cherries has always been to create an establishment where everyone feels welcome, providing excellent locally roasted coffees and fresh homemade delicious fare.
 
Over 20 years later Wild Cherries continues to be the place to be, a locale for all, where our baristas make you smile, our bustling kitchen makes you happy and the people you meet here will make your day.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Zano's Family Italian & Pizzeria
description: Looking for pizza in Truckee? Come to Zano’s! Create your own custom pizza or sample one of our specialties. We bake pizza for take home or for dining in our restaurant. Take home pizza is offered half-baked if you prefer. In addition to our delicious pizza, we serve incredible pasta, amazing salads and Italian family favorites including veal picatta, hearty lasagna and penne with meatballs.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: El Avagve Taqueria
description:
Jalisco is the home town for “El Agave Taqueria” owners, a town that is highly popular for its hard liquor “Tequila”, witch comes from the blue agave plant represented in the logo of this restaurant.

Tequila is a type of hard liquor which comes from a small region of Jalisco and which is made primarily from the blue agave plant. It is the most famous type of mezcal produced in Mexico, and the only mezcal which is produced industrially with strict standards. It is named after a small town northwest of the city of Guadalajara in the center of where it is produced and the native region of the blue agave. The plant was used in pre-Hispanic times to make a ceremonial drink. The Spanish used the sweet heart of the mature plant, called a piña (literally pineapple) to create a fermented and distilled beverage. The first person to have official permission to make and sell the liquor was José Antonio Cuervo in 1758. In 1888, the first license to export was given to the Sauza family.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: T-Bones Meat Shop
description:
Lorenzo grew up in the restaurant business. As a chef for many years, he understood the difference in quality from locally grown livestock. After moving to Northern California to he decided to open a butcher shop.

Jared and Lorenzo worked together at a previous restaurant, and Lorenzo knew that Jared would be an asset to the butcher shop operation.

After many years operating as a ranch/farm processing butcher shop they realized that a retail butcher shop was the logical next step. So they decided to partner up in the new venture of 

T-Bones Retail Meat Shop. Your neighborhood butcher. We look forward to serving you and our community for years to come.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Sunrise Cafe
description: Breakfast Cafe - Coffee- and Lunch service in Auburn, California. Burgers, Sandwich, Brunch, Eggs. Family owned and local fresh farmed food.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: The Grind
description: The Grind is a cozy eatery located in Auburn CA offering a warm atmosphere and a diverse menu that caters to all taste buds.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Gristmill
description: We are located at Wolf Road and Highway 49. Please stop by soon. Our hours of operation are Tuesday to Friday 7:00 AM to 3:00 PM and Saturdays 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM. We are available on Mondays by appointment only. Hours can change, so please keep an eye on the Gristmill facebook page for changes to our hours. We post our daily specials there, too!

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Montoliva Vinyard and Winery
description: Some of the original settlers of Chicago Park were first-generation Italian-Americans from Chicago, Illinois. The orchards and vineyards of Chicago Park bear witness to their quest to start a community in the untamed West that mirrored their homeland. Their spirit lives on at Montoliva Vineyard & Winery. Founded in 2000, Montoliva Vineyard & Winery creates Tuscan-inspired wines of uncommon depth and character.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Orchard Springs Campground
description: Orchard Springs Campground is in the Sierra Nevada Foothills, on Rollins Lake with beaches, a boat ramp, and boat rentals.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Cascade Shores General Store and Cafe
description: The store is the local hub of Cascade Shores. We are open year round, and offer basic supplies, groceries, craft beers and wine. Our Lazy Bear Cafe offers food 5 days a week. The store hours change for winter and summer.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: COMO Mexican Restaurant
description: At COMO, we believe in the power of handcrafted cuisine to bring people together. Every tortilla, hot sauce, and salsa we create is made from scratch because we know that the care and passion, we put into our food is what sets us apart. Join us at COMO, where every meal is a celebration of heritage and community.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Front Street Station Pizza
description:
Since 2004, Front Street Station Pizza Company has been a Tahoe City tradition for both locals and visitors.The Front Street family takes exceptional pride in serving some of Lake Tahoe area's very best, handcrafted food. 

Front Street Station's warm and welcoming log cabin structure on the banks of the Truckee River in Tahoe City, offer both a relaxed atmosphere and a cozy neighborhood feel. Everyone LOVES the New York-style pizza and craves the authentic Mexican specialties and mouth-watering sandwiches.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Good Wolf Brewing
description: The Good Wolf Brewing Company is a forest-inspired craft brewery in Truckee, CA, brewing imaginative beers with local and foraged ingredients.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: RMU Restaurant
description: RMU: Handcrafted skis & mountain gear for 2025, designed in the MTNS. Shop durable, recycled mountain bike packs and celebrate MTN culture.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Donner Creek Brewing
description:
We specialize in small batch, craft brew, good times & delicious grilled cheese panini-pressed sandwiches. We are family-and-dog-friendly, offer free pool with weekly tournaments on Monday nights and live music most Friday nights.

We LOVE living & playing in the beautiful Truckee outdoors & live for sharing our adventurous stoke with all of our guests! Come by & say "Hi" - it won't take long before you'll know why people call us the "Cheers of Truckee" :)

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Truckee Tavern
description: We believe a great dining experience must not only be made with fresh quality ingredients and be expertly prepared but it must invoke emotion and memory. Truckee Tavern’s food and beverage offerings include the freshest, highest quality local ingredients, farm raised, hormone free meats and locally grown produce to give a sense of place. Menu items showcase the incredible diversity of food available in California. The menus changes continually to reflect the seasons, changing tastes and availability to give the feel of moment. Retaining crowd favorites with new and exciting interpretations invokes anticipation in our regulars. These offerings are paired expertly with the finest wine, spirit and beer selections. Truckee Tavern’s spirit program draws from Truckee’s rich history of bootlegging during prohibition and focus on artisanal small batch gin and whiskey, the two main distillation products from Truckee during that era. This is complemented by a thoughtfully chosen selection of fine rum, vodka, brandy, tequila, mescal, liquors and cordials, all complimenting the overall experience.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Coffeebar Bakery
description:
Three locations in North Lake Tahoe! Don't let the name fool you - Coffeebar is more than just your average coffee shop. This radically-inclusive Italian Cafe - as they describe themselves - has the makings for breakfast, lunch, dinner and great conversation. Coffebar is known for holding strong beleifs in local, high quality food and sustainable business practices. 

Born out of an old tin garage and transformed into a modern style cafe, Coffeebar has everything from lattes and pastries to wine and quinoa bowls. Immerse yourself in authenicity at this attraction that features local art and culture.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Darkhorse Coffee Roasters Truckee
description: A place for crunchy dirtbags, local classics, academics, poets, philosophers, artists, angels, devils, mirrors, mystics, skeptics, rebels, those with money, those without, those lost, those found, leave your judgement at the door. Give a stranger a compliment. Try your coffee with no sweetener. Give the Dark Side a try. Take a Ride.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Drink Coffee Do Stuff
description: DRINK COFFEE DO STUFF is a specialty coffee company built with the belief that extraordinary coffee leads to an extraordinary life. The mantra began in the Swiss Alps in 2012 during founder Nick Visconti’s pro snowboard days and continues today at our Lake Tahoe headquarters. As specialty coffees grow at elevations similar to that of our roastery, all our coffees are sourced from the mountains and roasted in the mountains. The hallmark of our coffee roasting style is maximizing sweetness and revealing origin characteristic. High altitude roasting at 6,000ft in Lake Tahoe provides us unique advantages to achieve this consistently making all our coffees more sweet, less bitter.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Pacific Crest Coffee
description: We buy green beans from the best growing regions around the world, and roast them here at our headquarters in Truckee, CA.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Zuri Coffee Co.
description: Zuri Coffee Co. started as an ambitious plan for owners Esther and Werner Ospelt. Originally from Switzerland, the Ospelts set off on a journey to bring Swiss cafe charm from their youth to their new alpine home by creating the Zuri Coffee experience in Truckee, CA.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Clair Tappan Lodge
description: Venture out into nature and experience one of the most unique stays in our mountain lodge. We’re nestled amongst the trees where we have Donner Pass as a stunning backdrop to our property. With cozy accommodations and amenities to keep you busy, our lodge is the perfect place to stay for any traveler looking to get out into nature. We have unique event spaces, a convenient location close to Lake Tahoe, and a commitment to sustainability where we uphold the integrity of the natural environment around us. Welcome to Clair Tappaan Lodge, the best lodging near Lake Tahoe, CA.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Mi Pueblito Market
description: Truckee’s Mexican Market carries a variety of specialty items that are hard to find in other grocery stores. Try their marinated meats or pick up ingredients for authentic Mexican food.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Jerky Junction
description: 15 years ago we partnered with a local plant and trademarked our Tahoe Truckee Local Craft Jerky brand. Starting out as a small shop in the Iconic “Big Red Caboose” in Truckee selling Jerky, Candy, Gifts and more.  Covid saw us shutter the doors of the caboose as retail establishments struggled but our brand survived. We opened our own retail area in the restaurant and continued to grow our wholesale business into a regional powerhouse.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: New Moon Natural Foods
description: New Moon Natural Foods is a family-owned community market, serving North Tahoe with organic produce, locally-ranched meat, curated health & wellness products, unique beer, wine, & cheese selections, and all the best in natural grocery.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: The Truckee Food Shop
description:
High in the Sierras, you will find The Truckee Food Shop, a curated deli and market serving fresh food all year-round. Our food is prepared daily and sourced from organic farms, area ranchers, fishermen, and local co-ops. 

We offer a full take-out menu made from the seasonal bounty of California and Nevada ingredients.  We prepare delicious family-style “oven-ready” meals for any occasion. In addition to our regular menu, we offer weekly specials, made-to-order sandwiches, and farm-to-fork fare.  Please ask about our vegetarian and gluten-free options.  We also offer catering for private dinners and special events.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
assetType: Artisan Place to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Zander's The Bottleshop
description: Zander's The BottleShop is a high end Spirits shop where Spirit Guides help customers find beer, liquor and wine as well as cocktail mixers, bitters, and more.

sheet: Artisan Places to Eat, Drink or Stay
name: Zumo Wine
House of Fates
description:
Private Events - Theatre Productions
Protocols - Bisous - The Nymphiades · Anima · Gold Can’t Love You Back · Persephone’s Tale

name: InConcert Sierra
description: InConcert Sierra presents exceptional classical and choral performances that inspire and enhance our cultural life. We offer a warm, inclusive community for enjoying music and we provide educational opportunities for youth and adults.

name: LeGacy Presents
description:
LeGacy Presents is a small business theater company based in Nevada City, California. Dedicated to providing an affordable and meaningful theater experience for our community, we also provide opportunities to those who like to work hard, have fun and contribute to the quality of life in Nevada County and the surrounding regions.
We offer educational opportunities for those who want to expand their knowledge in the world of the performing arts and stagecraft- including Student Matinee's providing a unique theatrical experience for students of all ages!

name: Music in the Mountains
description: Our Mission is to inspire, engage, and connect our community through extraordinary musical experiences.

name: Nevada Theatre
description: The Nevada Theater is the oldest, continuosly operated theatre venue on the West Coast of the United States. Over 155 years of history, live performance, music, film, and theatre in Nevada County, CA.

name: Paul Emery Presents
description: Mr. Emery is a professional musician, producer and audio engineer (approx. 35 years). From 1998 to 2000, he contracted with Steve Lavere to restore vintage blues and jazz recordings. He has his own audio studio and audio production equipment (used for live sound, archiving, audio restoration, album/cd production and audio editing). He also owns a van used for equipment transport. Current clients, in addition to his own productions through Paul Emery Presents, include NC Chamber of Commerce, The Center for the Arts, Strings Concerts, KVMR, Nevada Theatre, Miners Foundry, Auburn Park and Recreation District, Alasdair Fraser, North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center, numerous bands, Legacy Productions, Oddfellows, GV Elks and others. In 2012, Emery was awarded the prestigious Dr. Leland and Sally Lewis Performing Arts award by the Nevada City Chamber of Commerce for his contribution and support of the performing arts in Nevada County.

name: Sierra Theatre Company
description: Sierra Theatre Company is proud to bring affordable, accessible, and high-quality theatre to Nevada County, CA. With a passionate and talented team at the helm, we are excited to carry on the legacy of delivering top-notch performances that our community has come to love and cherish.

name: The Cauldron
description: Inclusive witch's wellness center & apothecary. Herbs, supplies, & gifts curated w/ ❤️ & intention. Readings, classes, events, and more

name: The Lyric Rose Rose Theatre Company
description: The Lyric Rose Theatre is a non-profit (501c3) community theatre company dedicated to bringing great theatre and outreach through the performing arts.

name: The Onyx Theater
description: The Onyx Theatre - Where cinema meets creativity. Explore independent and foreign films that broaden horizons and ignite the imagination.

name: Air Aligned Aerial Arts Academy
description: We offer aerial arts for students as young as 18 months through adult

name: Center for the Arts
description: We are committed to bringing a diverse array of programming to western Nevada County, including music, dance, theater, film, visual art, comedy, youth arts education and literature.

name: Center Stage Dance Studio
description: Center Stage Dance Studio is a full spectrum dance and theatre studio offering classes in Ballet, Pointe, Tap, Jazz, Hip Hop, Contemporary, Pre Dance, Pilates, Children’s Theatre and more. Our experienced teaching staff creates a safe, supportive environment for every student to achieve their full potential.

name: Grass Valley Male Voice Choir
description: We are a group of male singers in the Sierra Foothills of California. We gather to share our love of music in the spirit of the Grass Valley Cornish gold miners of long ago.

name: Grass Valley Taiko
description: Grass Valley Taiko was established in 2000 by Founder and Artistic Director Mitzi Garnett from a basic one-time workshop that grew into the multiple classes of varied levels that exist now.

name: Music in the Mountains
description: Music in the Mountains inspires and engages community members of all ages by providing access to extraordinary classical music through performances and youth education programs.

name: Neighborhood Center of the Arts
description: Everyone needs a job, a place to find purpose and meaning in their life. For many Developmentally Disabled or Intellectually Challenged adults living in Nevada County, CA, that place is the Neighborhood Center of the Arts. We provide studios, materials and instruction in fiber arts, visual arts, clay, woodworking, jewelry, computers and performing arts as well as cooking, gardening and community outings.

name: Nevada County Fair
description: Located in Grass Valley, California, the Nevada County Fairgrounds has been declared as California’s Most Beautiful Fairgrounds, and is covered with hundreds of towering pine trees on almost 90 acres.  It is home to the annual Nevada County Fair, the Draft Horse Classic, the Country Christmas Faire, world class music festivals, and year round community events. There is also a year round RV Park, Lions Lake, rental facilities, weekly events, weddings, some of our community’s largest outdoor events, and lots of areas to walk.

name: Nevada County Fairgrounds Foundation Inc.
description: Our mission at the Nevada County Fairgrounds Foundation is simple: to build a better Nevada County Fairgrounds through fundraising and contributions to the Fairgrounds.

name: Nevada County Gem & Mineral Society
description: The Nevada County Gem and Mineral Society is a non profit organization that has the following objectives:  To promote the study of mineralogy, geology, and fossils.  To encourage the collection of minerals and gems. To foster the study and practice of the lapidary arts.  To provide field trips to mineral localities.  To promote good fellowship, education and recreation.

name: Nevada County Performing Arts Guild
description: We provide a quality Children’s Theatre Program for our Nevada County youth.

name: OLLI Orchestra
description: United by a love of music, the OLLI orchestra is a group of diverse musicians who rehearse and perform classical music. Some are retired professional musicians; some are completely new to orchestra.  The OLLI Orchestra provides an opportunity for musicians to extend and push themselves while having great fun playing with a group.

name: Sierra College Liberal Arts Departments
description: Since its first days, the promise of Sierra College has been to facilitate learning, inspire change, and build community. The College continues this mission as it adapts to meet the ever changing needs of students while promoting learning as a lifelong process.

name: Sierra Dance Institute
description: At Sierra Dance Institute, we offer a wide variety of dance classes for adults, teens, and children in a fun & vibrant atmosphere. We are located at the Grass Valley Center for the Arts in historic downtown Grass Valley.

name: Sierra Jazz Society
description: Our mission is to pass on the uniquely American tradition of jazz with the highest musical standards. Bill Douglass and the professional faculty share their knowledge and musical passion through annual summer Jazz Camp, concerts and workshops. Sierra Jazz Society, SJS, provides musical experiences that weave together the elements of jazz. Improvisation and discipline are basic and we combine them in an atmosphere of relaxed camaraderie and sympathetic communication.

name: Sierra Master Chorale/InConcert Sierra-Twin Cities Concert Association
description: InConcert Sierra presents exceptional classical and choral performances that inspire and enhance our cultural life. We offer a warm, inclusive community for enjoying music and we provide educational opportunities for youth and adults.

name: Sierra Stages Community Theater
description: Our mission is to enrich the cultural life of Nevada County by providing high quality, affordable and accessible performances for everyone. Over the past ten years, we have presented 36 productions of plays and musicals utilizing local talent onstage and behind the scenes at our community’s leading performing arts venues.

name: Prime Cinemas
description: Home of the Del Oro Theatre and Sutton Cinemas in Grass Valley, CA

name: Touchdown Productions
description: As a full service production company we can provide for all your video needs.

name: Crown Point Venues
description: Crown Point Venues provides opportunity and space for the development, creation, and presentation of cultural arts; and promotes and supports comprehensive arts education for the community of Nevada County, California, and the surrounding region.

name: Ananda Crystal Hermitage Gardens
description: Beautiful terraced gardens, expansive vistas, and peace of mind await all those who visit Crystal Hermitage.

name: Banner Mountain Artisans
description: Banner Mountain Artisans was started in 2004 by Joan Unter when she asked 16 creative artisans from Grass Valley, Penn Valley and Nevada City to offer their artwork at a holiday show. The first event was held in 6 homes in close proximity on Banner Mountain, Nevada City. More quality artists were added each year, and in 4 years the attendance exceeded all expectations and outgrew the neighborhood. Banner Mountain Artisans had built a reputation offering diverse, quality handmade gifts, all sold in beautiful homes among the towering trees.

name: Community of Writers
description: For 47 summers, the Community of Writers at Squaw Valley has brought together poets and prose writers for separate weeks of workshops, individual conferences, lectures, panels, readings, and discussions of the craft and the business of writing. Our aim is to assist writers in the refinement of their craft and thus, in an atmosphere of camaraderie and mutual support, move them closer to achieving their goals.

name: Foothill Country Dancers/Country Dance & Song Society
description: Foothill Country Dancers (FCD) is a small, volunteer-run non profit organization dedicated to promoting contra dancing in the foothills zone of Nevada and Placer counties.

name: Haute Trash an Artists Collaborative
description: The mission of Haute Trash is to create and produce performance art of the highest professional quality and to perform it before the broadest possible audience. We create fashion out of trash in order to entertain,educate and empower others to think,reuse and recycle.

name: Holt Ballet Conservatory
description: To offer systematic classical ballet curriculum from beginning levels to pre professional levels. To foster the artistic development of all students in the musicality and interpretation of ballet as a performing art. To deliver instruction based on the best current practices in safe physical training and practical performing skills. To mentor each student in personal goals in ballet, whether as a profession, a college scholarship and degree, or a rewarding hobby. To create dancers who move with joy and passion.

name: LeGacy Productions
description: LeGacy Productions is a family owned and operated small business theater company based in Nevada County, California. We are dedicated to providing an affordable and meaningful theater experience for our community.   We also provide an opportunity to those who like to work hard, have fun and contribute to the quality of life in Nevada County and the surrounding regions.

name: Miners Foundry Cultural Center
description: The Miners Foundry Cultural Center is a living historic treasure serving Nevada County as a cultural arts and community events center.  More than a venue, the nonprofit’s mission is to preserve, enhance and utilize the historic Miners Foundry for cultural, educational and social activities in Nevada County.

name: Nevada City Ballet Academy
description: A studio that is committed to providing careful, correct ballet training to students in a safe, non-competitive and comfortable atmosphere,

name: Nevada City Film Festival
description: NCFF screens almost 100 films, including comedies, dramas, documentaries, animation, and international shorts, in a variety of historic locations in downtown Nevada City. By combining creative programming of short and full length films, enthusiastic audiences and a truly festive environment, NCFF has become known as one of the most innovative, fun and artist-friendly events on the film festival circuit. In addition to creating a one of a kind experience for both the audience and filmmaker, NCFF offers terrific opportunities to network and forum.

name: Nevada County Arts Council
description: We facilitate collaborative efforts that promote and sustain the visual, literary and performing arts of Nevada County to advance the cultural, social and economic life of our community

name: Nevada County Composers Cooperative
description: The Nevada County Composers Cooperative exists to foster the creation and performance of new original music

name: Nevada Union Art Guild
description: Nevada Union Art Guild is a collaborative association of students, faculty, and parents that exists to maintain and promote the Nevada Union High School Fine Art Department.

name: North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center
description: The mission of the North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center is to inspire and educate our community by providing a venue for gatherings, performance, art and education that enriches and sustains our rural character and traditions.

name: Off Broadstreet Theatre
description: FUN is the key word at Off Broad St. Our goal is to bring you an unforgettable evening of entertainment. We produce only comedies, and most of these are musicals, with a slant toward familiar hit songs of the 1950s and 60s. Intimate cabaret seating and a relaxed and friendly atmosphere add to the uniqueness of the experience.

name: Paul Emery Productions
description: Paul Emery is a musician, producer and audio engineer.  For 41 years he has been producing concerts and gatherings that create unforgettable moments for both the performers and attendees.

name: Sierra Fiddle Camp
description: Alasdair Fraser's Sierra Fiddle Camp in northern California at Shady Creek Camp on the San Juan Ridge (near Nevada City) invites you to a week of intensive study, recreation, and good times with people who share an interest in the music and dance traditions of Scotland and beyond

name: The Curious Forge
description: The Curious Forge is a nonprofit 501(c)3 and LLC MakerSpace located at 13024 Bitney Springs Rd in Nevada City, California. With over 20,000 sq feet of space, members have access to training and a wide variety of over $100,000 in equipment from traditional to advanced. Most of all we are a community of builders, tinkerers, artists and professionals who love to make. Think of it as a artistic, technical and industrial playground.

name: The Upstart Theater Company
description: Formed in 2017 as a profit sharing collective, we are experienced theatre makers who have previously worked together many times. Come see our shows!

name: Community Asian Theatre of the Sierra (CATS)
description: The Community Asian Theatre of the Sierra (CATS), a 501c(3) public benefits charitable corporation, promotes diversity through the arts through multicultural theater, events and workshops.

name: Film Projects
description: We Are a spiritually focused, culturally diverse and socially conscious independent film company. Addressing the wounds inflicted by institutionalized stereotypes and fundamentalist doctrines and beliefs.

name: Lake Wildwood Music Club
description: The Lake Wildwood Community Association charters and supports over 40 different Member Clubs. Examples include Tennis, Pickleball, Bocce Ball, Bridge, Collectabelles, Computers (Mac & PC), Men's and Women's Golf, Fishing, Arts, Gardening, Motorcycle, Music, Ping Pong, Pinochle, Sewing & Quilting, Singles, Spanish Dancing, Theater, Video Production, Water Ski & Water Sports, and Women's Chorus. Many other clubs and groups also exist at Lake Wildwood. Some of these clubs are open to those who are not residents of Lake Wildwood. If interested in joining a club, Members can find contact information with their log in. Nonresidents can email info@lwwa.org and be put in touch with the club's representative.

name: Lake Wildwood Theatre
description: The Lake WIldwood Theater has been producing 2 major productions a year for the last 30 years including musicals, dramas and comedies.

name: Lake Wildwood Womens’ Chorus
description: The Lake Wildwood Community Association charters and supports over 40 different Member Clubs. Examples include Tennis, Pickleball, Bocce Ball, Bridge, Collectabelles, Computers (Mac & PC), Men's and Women's Golf, Fishing, Arts, Gardening, Motorcycle, Music, Ping Pong, Pinochle, Sewing & Quilting, Singles, Spanish Dancing, Theater, Video Production, Water Ski & Water Sports, and Women's Chorus. Many other clubs and groups also exist at Lake Wildwood. Some of these clubs are open to those who are not residents of Lake Wildwood. If interested in joining a club, Members can find contact information with their log in. Nonresidents can email info@lwwa.org and be put in touch with the club's representative.

name: Penn Valley Community Rodeo Association
description: The Penn Valley Community Rodeo Association is a non profit corporation, founded in 2005 to promote the sport of rodeo in Penn Valley, and to preserve the western lifestyle. The PVCRA benefits local schools and service clubs. PVCRA awards two scholarships each year to students in Agriculture.

name: Inner Rhythms
description: Using the power of dance and music to cultivate life skills in our youth, promote global stewardship and create an everlasting passion for the arts.

name: Sierra College Tahoe Truckee
description: Since its first days, the promise of Sierra College has been to facilitate learning, inspire change, and build community. The College continues this mission as it adapts to meet the ever-changing needs of students while promoting learning as a lifelong process.

name: Sierra Dance Center
description: Whether young or old, a beginner or advanced dancer, we offer dance unique methods of dance training that will help you take your dancing to new heights.

name: Tahoe Truckee School of Music
description: The Tahoe Truckee School of Music offers an opportunity in our community for learning and sharing music. We encourage those who are new to music as well as those who are experienced musicians. We focus on an environment that serves as a positive outlet not only for learning, but creativity, fun and interaction with others. We create and share music

name: Trails and Vistas
description: Trails & Vistas' mission is to create community by celebrating the arts and nature. We facilitate art events in nature for people of all ages to promote an appreciation of the performing and visual arts, long lasting environmental stewardship, and create relationships between the artists, the attendees and the local and world community.

name: Truckee Arts Alliance
description: Truckee Arts Alliance represents the voice and clearinghouse for arts and culture in the Truckee community.

name: Truckee Community Theater
description: The mission of Truckee Community Theater is to create opportunities for community participation in theatrical offerings of the highest possible quality presented to audiences at affordable prices.  We are dedicated to providing theatrical training to residents of all ages through plays, workshops, camps, and classes, to offer arts education that promotes life long learning, to collaborate with other local arts organizations, and to celebrate the power of theater to illuminate our common humanity.

name: Truckee Dance Factory Booster Club
description: Dance is our passion, our business, our life and it is our goal to inspire others to grow their own passion for the art of dance.  We strive to nurture each and every student and to give them the physical, mental and emotional tools to uncover their full potential as dancers and artists.  Truckee Dance Factory promotes strong self confidence, self-respect, discipline and commitment.  We encourage each dancer to find their own creativity and vision in order to excel in their art.

name: Truckee High School Bands
description: Truckee High School Bands has several ensembles including: Wind Ensemble, Symphonic Band, Marching Band, Jazz Combo, Jazz Band and Jazz Ensemble.

name: Truckee Public Arts Commission
description: TPAC, the Truckee Public Art Commission, focuses on creating opportunities for public art, installation art, exhibits, and art events for the Community.

name: Truckee Roundhouse Makerspace
description: The roundhouse is a non profit makerspace that supports the teaching, learning, and practicing of a wide variety of crafts skills, technologies and arts in the Truckee Tahoe  community.

name: Truckee Tahoe Community Chorus  Sierra College Campus
description: The mission of the Truckee Tahoe Community Chorus is to perform a wide range of quality choral works that will appeal to the diverse community members of the Truckee and North Lake Tahoe region. TTCC is dedicated to building, nurturing and promoting a strong choral musical arts program in the schools and within the community.

name: Ivy Photography
description: Alicia Berardi of Ivy Photography is a portrait artist specializing in families, seniors, and maternity.

name: Inspire Woodworks
description: Inspire Woodworks is a family owned business run by Danny and Tina Garner. Together they create one of a kind pieces that they choose to fractal burn or create a beautiful ocean or enhance the beauty of the natural wood. Danny and Tina infuse their inspiration into every piece they create, while seamlessly matching function with beauty for a stunning result. Their functional art ranges from furniture, bar tops, beer taps and tasting boards, charcuterie boards and more!

name: Tahoe Music Alive
description: Live music has the power to connect, uplift, and transform. Every Donation, no matter the size, helps us bring world‑class musicians to Tahoe, keep concerts accessible to families, and inspire young people through music.

name: KidZone Museum
description: The KidZone Museum is a children’s museum in Truckee, California that serves 30,000 visits annually. Our mission is to inspire learning through creative play and discovery. Our exhibits are optimal for children under 7. Our camps and school programs are designed for children through elementary school. Teenagers can gain important service learning experience through volunteering at the museum.

name: Encompass Films
description: We are a full-service production company that specializes in using adventure and the outdoors to tell deep and meaningfuls. I could tell you about our award-winning success and our ability to handle anything you may have dreamt up, but I’ll save you the tedium and tell you this instead...

name: Truckee Tahoe Arts Alliance
description: Truckee Tahoe Arts Alliance (TTAA) unites artists and arts organizations from across the Truckee/Tahoe region to collaborate, create, and elevate the local arts community. Together, we inspire, support, and celebrate the power of the arts in Nevada County.

name: Tahoe Truckee Pride Foundation
description: We love living and playing in the incredible landscape of the Sierra Nevada. Historically, rural towns and outdoor spaces haven't always been accepting of the queer community. We’re working to make sure that regardless of sexual orientation, gender identity, race and lifestyle, everybody feels safe, supported and welcome in the Truckee area.

name: Truckee Tahoe Youth Chorus
description: To enrich the hearts and minds of our community by learning, performing, and sharing choral music

name: Truckee Community Theater
description: Truckee Community Theater's mission is to provide community members of all ages with an inclusive space to engage in high quality performing arts, inspire creativity, and bring our community together to experience the transformative magic of live theater.

name: South Nevada County Chamber of Commerce
description: The Chamber’s mission statement is to provide a voice for the business community and enhance the economic vitality of South Nevada County. As our membership continues to grow and flourish, the Chamber will continue to work with our local businesses and the community, bringing vitality to the South Nevada County area.

name: Booktown Books
description: Booktown is a cooperative of 14 independent book sellers with 4,000 square feet of Used and Rare Books, Ephemera, Books on CDs, Music CDs, DVDs, LP Records and Art. We have added a new Rare Book Room where discriminating collectors can find fabulous rare, unusual and antiquarian books.

name: Golden Road Radio and Television
description: The Golden Road Radio and Television Show explores the intersection of the arts and culture, the environment, health, peace and justice and science and technology. We are dedicated to building a better future through education, individual empowerment, community building and positive social change.

name: Grass Valley Chamber of Commerce
description: To serve our members by facilitating business development, promoting tourism and visitor direction and presenting information that supports responsible business growth in our community.

name: Grass Valley Downtown Association
description: The Grass Valley Downtown Association shall represent its general membership with a unified voice in economic development and historical preservation of downtown Grass Valley and our community

name: Grass Valley Library Royce Branch
description: The Grass Valley Library - Royce Branch is a wonderfully historic Carnegie library listed in the National Register of Historic Places.

name: KNCO / Star Radio
description: Nevada County’s first choice for local news.

name: Nevada Union High School
description: Nevada Union has more to offer than any other comprehensive high school in the region.  There is something here for everyone, and each provides an opportunity to shine. Whether it's academics, sports, dance, choir, AG metals, farming, drama, band, or woodworking,  the list goes on.

name: Sierra College
description: Since its first days, the promise of Sierra College has been to facilitate learning, inspire change, and build community. The College continues this mission as it adapts to meet the ever changing needs of students while promoting learning as a lifelong process.

name: The Book Seller
description: We are a full-service independent bookstore located in historic downtown Grass Valley, the heart of California's Gold Country.

name: The Union Newspaper
description: The Union is is one of the oldest daily newspapers in California. It was founded in 1864, as the Civil War raged and people in Nevada County were divided. The Union made its stand clear: For The Union, One and Inseparable. Those words have appeared on its front page every day since then.

name: Community Legal
description: Community Legal, Inc. (CLI) is a non-profit organization located in Nevada City, California, that provides free and low-cost legal services to low-income residents of Nevada County.

name: Economic Resource Council
description: The NCERC is committed to ensuring a thriving, attractive business environment in Nevada County. We support scalable ventures that create quality jobs, promote entrepreneurship, help existing businesses, attract new businesses, and contribute to the overall prosperity of the community.  Our organization provides a platform for dialogue and collaboration to create strong, informed strategies for Nevada County’s economic ecosystem.

name: Friends of Nevada County Libraries
description: Founded in 1972, Friends of Nevada County Libraries is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to supporting and improving library services in Nevada County, California.

name: Hardy Books
description: Hardy Books is a rare and out-of-print book and ephemera seller located in Nevada City, California, in the heart of the California Northern Mines Gold Country.   We specialize in Western Americana, "All Things Californian" (California History, Photographers, Poets, Authors, Law & Order, San Francisco, Yosemite, Monterey Peninsula, and so forth), Western Fine Press, Books to Film, Signed Books and Books On Books, as well as original Posters, Movie Memorabilia and the odd item of special interest or value.  We list only a small part of our inventory on line, so please contact us if you are looking for a particular item.

name: Harmony Books
description: Nevada City's complete bookstore for all your reading enjoyment.

name: Hour Nevada County
description: Hour Nevada County is an hour exchange based on the equality of time, empowering individuals to utilize their assets and enhance their lives, neighborhood and community.

name: KVMR
description: KVMR provides community members opportunities to connect through the development and production of music, news, and public affairs programs that entertain, inform, and educate.

name: Nevada City Chamber of Commerce
description:
The non-profit Nevada City Chamber of Commerce represents the interests of the local business community in Nevada City, California. The chamber is essential in fostering economic development, fighting for policies that are pro-business, and aiding small businesses in the area.

The Nevada City Chamber of Commerce’s promotion of the community as a tourist and visitor destination is one of its main responsibilities. To develop marketing campaigns that highlight Nevada City’s distinctive culture, history, and natural beauty, the chamber collaborates closely with neighborhood businesses, lodging facilities, and tourist destinations.

name: Nevada City Rotary Club
description: The Nevada City Rotary Club is the service club people want to join because it’s a diverse and welcoming group of people who live Service Above Self by giving financial and hands-on support to our local, national and international communities, exemplify ethical leadership, and get things done while having good, clean fun.

name: Nevada City School of the Arts
description: We see our role as facilitators of the Arts in the community, enriching it with dance and theater, exhibitions and performances, collaborating with other artists, promoting and assisting the vision of arts-based education with other educational entities.

name: Nevada County Digital Media Center
description: The Nevada County Digital Media Center is Nevada County’s hub for all things film & video. Creation, Collaboration, Learning & Distribution!

name: Madelyn Helling Library
description: In August 1972, the Nevada County Library System was incorporated, with the Nevada City library serving as the main branch.  Today the system includes the Grass Valley Library, the Truckee Library. the Doris Foley Library for Historical Research. The Bear River Library (a partnership with the Bear River High School Library) and Penn Valley Library (located in a shopping center) were added in 2002.

name: Nevada County Television/Digital Media Ctr
description: To foster community dialogue, increase civic engagement, and enrich the cultural life of our community through television, the internet, and other digital media.

name: Searls Historical Library
description: The Searls Historical Library is the archive of the Nevada County Historical Society. Established in 1973, the Searls Library has become one of the premier libraries of materials on the area's history, people and mines which contributed to the development of this county.

name: Sierra FoodWineArt Magazine
description: Sierra FoodWineArt is a free, quarterly lifestyle magazine that is the go-to publication for restaurants, wineries, wine & beer, arts & music, and travel & tourism in the Sierra Foothills, Tahoe/Truckee, Reno and outlying Sacramento region. We also have a companion website called SierraCulture.com.

name: Sierra Harvest
description: Sierra Harvest’s mission is to educate, inspire, and connect Nevada County families to fresh, local, seasonal food.

name: The Center for Non Profit Leadership
description: The Center for Nonprofit Leadership (CNL) is a resource center for organizations and individuals in Nevada County and the Sierra Nevada region of California. Founded in 2003 as a catalyst to strengthen and sustain local nonprofit organizations, CNL focuses on best practice workshops, executive leadership seminars, on-site coaching and mentoring, and public advocacy.

name: KYRR 93.3 FM
description: KYRR is a licensed non-profit public safety community radio station operated by Yuba River Radio, a community radio club, broadcasting 24/7 programs as diverse as heavy metal, trance, world, classic rock, punk, hip-hop, and pop music with a focus on local artists.

name: Activities & Improvement Center
description: Our Mission is to eliminate barriers to full equality for people with disabilities through programs which promote independent living and effect systems change, while honoring dignity and self determination.

name: Penn Valley Chamber of Commerce
description: Our mission is to promote and stimulate business growth and development to benefit area businesses and the communities they serve. To facilitate our mission, we are continually broadening our media outreach through community involvement and radio, TV, and newspaper coverage, in addition to application of the Internet and distribution of electronic media.

name: Penn Valley Community Foundation
description: Dedicated to establishing a Penn Valley Cultural Center, a centrally located facility for all ages and interests.

name: Rough and Ready Chamber of Commerce
description: Rough and Ready is located in Nevada County, California, just a short drive west of Grass Valley.  Rough and Ready provides ideal country living with proximity to outdoor recreational activities, and yet we are reasonably close to metropolitan areas.

name: Boys and Girls Club of North Lake Tahoe
description: Our mission is to inspire and enable all youth, especially those who need us most, to reach their full potential as productive, caring, responsible citizens.

name: Community Collaborative of Tahoe Truckee
description: The Community Collaborative of Tahoe Truckee (CCTT) is a partnership of nonprofit and public organizations working together to address fundamental needs of families in the Tahoe Truckee Region. Collectively, we identify emerging community issues and develop strategies with our combined vision and resources.

name: Family Resource Center of Truckee
description: To promote social and economic success in our community by providing education, mobilizing resources and advocating for change.

name: Friends of the Truckee Library
description: Friends of the Truckee Library is a nonprofit organization dedicated to promoting and providing support for the Truckee Library as an essential institution of society. Our members advocate, educate and raise funds on behalf of the library. We are a dedicated group of active volunteers that have spent decades devoted to growing and sustaining the Truckee Library.

name: Grant Kaye Photography
description: I lead small (less than 6) groups of keen photographers on guided workshops to destinations around the globe. My trips are custom tailored to offer the perfect balance of adventure, photography, and learning

name: High Fives Foundation
description: The High Fives Foundation supports the dreams of outdoor action sports athletes by raising injury prevention awareness while providing resources and inspiration to those who suffer life altering injuries.

name: Kitchen Collab
description: We are a collaborative kitchen space and culinary incubator available for rent to caterers, private chefs, food production companies, food trucks, food educators, and health professionals. Our Mission is to elevate and strengthen the Truckee Tahoe food economy by supporting its food entrepreneurs.

name: KTKE Radio 101.5 FM
description: Our Mission is to entertain and inform the local community. We are independent so we can play and talk about what you want to hear. Our format is Triple A, which means we play a wide variety of music.

name: Moonshine Ink
description: Moonshine Ink brings you Tahoe news straight from the source. Our team of hardworking journalists uncovers North Tahoe and Truckee news stories with in depth reporting and our authentic for the community, by the community spirit.

name: Sierra Business Council
description: Sierra Business Council pioneers and demonstrates innovative approaches and solutions to increase community vitality, economic prosperity, environmental quality, and social fairness in the Sierra Nevada.

name: Tahoe Donner Homeowners Association
description: Tahoe Donner is a recreational oriented mountain residential community whose mutual benefit association of 6,500 owners provides for the standards, regular operation and long term maintenance of programs, facilities and open space.

name: Tahoe Safe Alliance
description: Our mission is to end the incidence and trauma of intimate partner and domestic violence, sexual violence, and child abuse in North Lake Tahoe and Truckee.

name: Tahoe Truckee Community Foundation
description: Tahoe Truckee Community Foundation connects people and opportunities, generating resources to build a more caring, creative, and effective community.

name: Town of Truckee
description: Creating the best future for our community requires the highest level of public service every day. We will accomplish that in partnership with the Town’s citizens and our fellow governmental agencies.

name: Truckee Chamber of Commerce
description: Our mission is to promote and develop local business and tourism while improving the community's quality of life.

name: Truckee Downtown Merchants Association
description: The vision of Truckee Downtown Merchants Association (TDMA) is to create, support and sustain a vibrant and visible downtown through solid membership, community collaboration and unique events; ensuring a vital, art-filled and welcoming authentic Historic Downtown Truckee for residents and visitors alike.

name: Truckee Tahoe Community Television
description: The mission of Truckee Tahoe Community Television is to provide a PEG Access Television Center to the residents of Truckee and North Tahoe, CA at limited cost.

name: Word after Word Books
description: Word After Word Books is a welcoming space for the community and visitors to connect around books. The Word After Word Team hosts author events, writing workshops, book clubs, and story times.

name: Truckee Tahoe Community Television (TTCTV)
description: At Truckee Tahoe Community Television our mission is to foster community awareness and communication through local programming from individuals, groups and agencies. We serve the Truckee, North Tahoe area with interesting and informative programs through Suddenlink Channel 6, Channel 18 and online video streaming. We instruct the general public, and TTUSD students in particular, in the motion picture arts and sciences.

name: Bear River Library
description: The Bear River Library is located on Combie Road in the Lake Center Plaza near the entrance to the Lake of the Pines.

name: Sierra Community House
description: On July 1, 2019, four long-standing non-profit, social service organizations — Family Resource Center of Truckee, North Tahoe Family Resource Center, Tahoe SAFE Alliance, and Project MANA — united to form Sierra Community House. Sierra Community House is a 501c3, non-profit organization. Our goal is to better serve our region, share resources, leverage partnerships, and connect individuals and families with the services they need to thrive to build the strength, safety, and resilience of the community.

name: La Fuerza Latina Agency Truckee
description: We are La Fuerza Latina Agency, a women of color-owned marketing agency rooted in community, culture, and purpose. Our journey began in our hometown of Truckee, California, where we grew up together and were both active members of La Fuerza Latina, a college access program dedicated to empowering underrepresented youth on their path to higher education. Though life took us to opposite coasts with Leslie earning her degree from Wesleyan University in the East, and Marlyn graduating from CSU Chico in the West, our shared passion for community brought us back home.

name: Tahoe Truckee Media
description: Tahoe Truckee Media is your local educational and government media center. We are here to provide a venue for the local voice, to educate, and to keep residents up to date on local government and events.

name: Prime Cinemas Del Oro Theatre
description: Discover Prime Cinemas - your local movie destination. Come for the films, stay for the memories.

name: Nevada County Doris Foley Library for Historical Research
Nevada County Library Bear River Branch
Nevada County Library Grass Valley Royce Branch
Nevada County Library Madelyn Helling Branch
Nevada County Library Penn Valley Branch
4th of July Parade
description: The 4th of July celebration and parade alternates each year between downtown Grass Valley and downtown Nevada City.

name: 4th of July Celebration Fireworks
description: Following the parade, there will be a celebration under the pines at the Nevada County Fairgrounds. Live music, entertainment, fireworks, food and refreshments are offered.

name: Celtic Festival
description: First founded in 1996, the KVMR Celtic Festival includes eleven performance areas including five musical and six demonstration stages and a weekend long youth arts instruction that has included Celtic music, magic, dance and songwriting.

name: Cornish Christmas
description: Cornish Christmas started in 1967 as a way to preserve Grass Valley’s Cornish heritage and holiday traditions. Mill and West Main Streets are closed to motorized traffic and filled with the sights and sounds of an old fashioned Christmas; carolers, jugglers, musicians, magicians, the Grass Valley Cornish Carol Choir, Tommyknocker Cloggers and of course, Santa Claus. Enjoy delicious food and drink while perusing handmade arts and crafts from artists throughout California.

name: Country Christmas Craft Fair
description: Grass Valley hosts a major Country Christmas Faire at the Nevada County Fairgrounds with 100+ artisans, Santa, bonfire, music, and crafts.

name: Cruzin' in the Pines Car Show
description: One of the best classic car shows in Northern California.

name: Draft Horse Classic
description: The Classic has grown to become the premier Draft Horse show in the western United States, and features six majestic performances, breathtaking competitions and entertainment in between classes.

name: Earth's Treasures Gem & Mineral Show
description: Check out the gem and mineral collections, visit the booths of the numerous vendors, bid on minerals and rocks at a silent auction, watch local artisans at work, and see the demonstrations. This show is a great place to meet other enthusiasts, buy supplies, and experience everything the rock and mineral hobby has to offer.

name: Fall Colors Open Studio Tour  (host:Center for the Arts)
description: The Center for the Arts, with support from the Nevada County Arts Council, presents the Fall Colors Open Studio Tour.  Over 70 local artists welcome the public to 47 studios and art galleries during a free tour spanning two weekends in October.

name: Fathers Day Bluegrass Festival
description: California Bluegrass Association presents a legendary bluegrass, old time, and gospel music festival over four days in June.

name: Foothills Celebration
description: The Foothills Celebration is just the therapy you’ll need for a late winter weekend.  The wineries and restaurants are all paired with local downtown Grass Valley merchants who open their stores for this unique wine and food tasting affair.

name: Grass Valley Car Show
description: Travel back in time and experience American nostalgia at its best. See approximately 300 of the finest custom, vintage and antique cars and trucks in all of Northern California; in a city that honors its historic past and embraces its wonderful future: Historic Downtown Grass Valley.

name: Grass Valley Farmers Market
description: The Grass Valley Thursday Night Markets are a wonderful community event that includes a certified Farmer’s Market and a whole lot more including arts, crafts, food, and the ever popular FREE Summer Concert Series.

name: Grass Valley Old West Antique Show
description: The annual Old West Antiques Show provides the opportunity to view and purchase world class merchandise from a legendary list of dealers in all collecting categories, including museum quality Antique Firearms, Native American items, Western & Cowboy Memorabilia, Advertising, Coin Operated & Gambling, Saloon & General Store, Paintings, Bronzes, Lamps, Gas & Oil, Jewelry, Furniture and more!

name: Holidays at the Empire Mine
description: Celebrate the holidays early 1900s’ style at Holidays at Empire Mine.  With old fashioned decorations, lavish period costumes, and festive live music, Empire Cottage will be brimming with good cheer. Docents will share insights about Empire’s glory days when it ranked as one of the most prosperous hard-rock gold mines in North American history

name: Miners Picnic
description: The annual Miners Picnic is a summer celebration of our colorful, gold mining history.
