The first Miners Picnic was held in 1895 to raise money for miners’ widows and orphans, as well as injured and out of work miners. Today, it’s an action packed day of fun, food, gold history, and lasting memories.

name: Music in the Mountains Summer Fest
description: For more than 37 years, Music in the Mountains  SummerFest has been Northern California’s premier summer concert series, featuring exciting outdoor performances and sophisticated chamber concerts, demonstrating that classical music, performed by a world class orchestra and chorus, can transform lives and communities.

name: Nevada County Certified Growers Market
description: Nevada County's original certified market, bringing growers and the community together since 1980.

name: Nevada County Fair
description: Five days of excitement and fun for Fair goers of all ages. Enjoy live entertainment, delicious food, carnival rides, animals, and exhibits in a community friendly environment and at affordable family prices!

name: Sierra BrewFest
description: A unique, unlimited microbrew tasting experience. Sample over 60 microbrews and a no host wine bar for those who would prefer wine over beer!  This year features even more delicious food from some of the best local food trucks and restaurants.

name: Sierra Poetry Festival
description: In addition to its mainstage event, Sierra Poetry Festival marks National Poetry Month from the rolling foothills of California’s Gold Country to the rugged High Sierra, bringing our rich literary community together to celebrate the spoken word and reach out to brand new audiences in fresh ways. Readings, workshops, music, an activity fair for all ages and youth performances will be preceded by a month of pre festival pop-up poetry events.

name: St. Piran's Day Festival
description: Every year Grass Valley captures some fun with its annual St. Piran’s Day pasty tossing contest. The rain or shine event, which celebrates the Cornish immigrants who settled around the gold mines, is fun for adults, children and especially dogs.

name: Strawberry Music Festival
description: The Strawberry Music Festival presents the gold standard in main stage entertainment, intimate side stage performances, top notch children’s programming, workshops and activities for the whole family, and some of the nicest people you will ever meet. Programs like the Breakfast Club and the Band Scramble, live broadcasts by Hog Ranch Radio, and Strawberry’s legendary camp jam culture are what makes this your festival.

name: Thursday Night Markets
description: Every Thursday evening, our charming streets come alive with an eclectic mix of artisan vendors, farm-fresh produce, sizzling street food, and toe-tapping live music.Wander through local maker booths, sip something cool, and catch up with friends old and new. From handmade crafts and gourmet treats to face painting and family fun, there’s something for everyone. Whether you’re a long-time local or a first-time visitor, this is summer in Grass Valley at its best—vibrant, friendly, and unforgettable.

name: Craft Guild Winterfair
description: Newly inspired artisan and craft event, featuring many new artisans and crafters just in time for holiday shopping.

name: Red White and Country Music Festival
description: The Red, White & Country Music Festival takes place at the Nevada County Fairgrounds for an all-day concert experience packed with live music, food, and family-friendly fun!

name: Firebird (summerfest)
description: Stravinsky’s Firebird Suite along with Edward Elgar’s Cello Concerto in E minor, Op. 85, Jessie Montgomery’s Starburst, and Jennifer Higdon’s Blue Cathedral.

name: Chinese New Year Festival & Parade
description: The Festival and Parade honors the early Chinese pioneers of the Sierra.

name: Constitution Day Parade
description: Nevada City’s Constitution Day Parade, a local tradition since 1967, is reported to be the oldest and largest Constitution observance in western America.   The parade through the downtown historic district includes marching bands, floats, antique autos, equestrians, politicians and the perennial crowd favorites the Famous Marching Presidents of Nevada City.

name: First Friday Art Walk
description: The First Friday Art Walk is a celebration of Nevada City’s diverse mix of galleries, artisan boutiques, retail shops, wine tasting rooms, restaurants, as well as our wonderfully artistic and creative community.

name: Mardis Gras Parade
description: The 27th anniversary of Nevada City’s annual Mardi Gras Celebration is set to happen on Sunday, February 17, 2019. The annual event, hosted by the Nevada City Chamber of Commerce, features a colorful & festive parade & street faire throughout the streets of historic downtown Nevada City.

name: Nevada City Craft Fair
description: Nevada City Craft Fair is a juried exhibition of makers curated in the aesthetics of the modern rustic lifestyle that NCCF embodies. Hip, sustainable, yet innovative, represents the best of the region in our bi annual craft fair held at the historic Miners Foundry in downtown Nevada City.

name: Nevada City Farmers Market
description: Each Saturday from June to November more than 30 vendors join a group of loyal volunteers to create a vibrant community event on Union Street in downtown Nevada City.

name: Nevada City Film Festival
description: NCFF screens almost 100 films, including comedies, dramas, documentaries, animation, and international shorts, in a variety of historic locations in downtown Nevada City. By combining creative programming of short and full length films, enthusiastic audiences and a truly festive environment, NCFF has become known as one of the most innovative, fun and artist friendly events on the film festival circuit.

name: Nevada City Summer Nights
description: The Summer Nights in Nevada City celebration, a street festival of art and music, has been an annual tradition in this quaint, scenic, historic (and sometimes quirky) community, for close to 30 years. This free event takes place on three Wednesday evenings in July, when  Nevada City’s landmark historic district is closed to motorized traffic and filled with arts, crafts, classic cars, food, drink and music. Leading Sierra foothills musicians perform on outdoor stages throughout the downtown area.

name: The Childrens’ Festival
description: The Children’s Festival has become one of the premiere summer events for children, with over 2000 children and adults attending. All who attend leave with fond memories and many of the children who attended in the 1970’s and 80’s are now returning with their own children. A very special event that brings the community together to celebrate the magic of imagination.

name: Victorian Christmas
description: The enticing aromas of roasted chestnuts and hearty holiday foods fill the air, along with cries of street vendors hawking their wares and lamp lit streets filled to overflowing with handcrafted candy, jewelry, pottery, perfume, dolls, and over 100 different items in all.  This annual, family tradition takes place 2nd Wednesday evenings and 3rd Sunday afternoons in December and features holiday activities for all ages.

name: Wild & Scenic Environmental Film Festival
description: One of the nation’s premier environmental and adventure film festivals, the 17th annual event will feature films that combine stellar filmmaking, beautiful cinematography and first-rate storytelling to inform, inspire and ignite solutions and possibilities to restore the earth. Each year, the Wild & Scenic Film Festival draws top filmmakers, celebrities, leading activists, social innovators and well-known world adventurers for film, music, workshops, celebrations, and inspiration.

name: Earth Fest Street Fair
description: This event is sure to inform and inspire all who attend and will include live music, special performances, interactive booths and demos, kid’s activities, earth-friendly vendors, organic foods, and more.

name: Humbug Day
description: This annual “hometown homecoming” celebration brings North Bloomfield’s history to life, with volunteers in period costume interpreting the buildings and cultural heritage of the gold rush semi-ghost town that was first called Humbug.

name: Sierra Storytelling Festival
description: Known as the Queen of Storytelling Festivals, our festival brings a lineup of talented, nationally recognized storytellers to the Schoolhouse stages. People have told stories since the beginning of time and our storytellers keep the tradition alive, voicing the narratives of life, our histories and traditions, our reasons for living, our hopes and dreams.

name: Penn Valley Rodeo
description: PVCRA produces quality family entertainment in the western tradition with two nights of pro rodeo, Friday and Saturday.  Events include AMA sanctioned Mule & Donkey Show, the Doc Halstead Open Barrel Race series, and Summer Fun Gymkhana Buckle series.

name: Secession Days
description: On the first Sunday of June, Rough and Ready celebrates Secession Days with a special Chili Cookoff.  Guests can taste and vote for their favorite chili along with the judges.   The Original Saga of Rough and Ready melodrama, Blacksmith demonstrations in the historic Fippin Blacksmith Shop, a variety of vendors and crafters, music, additional food options from the local Community Hall and Firefighter's Association, raffles, souvenir sales, water slide and bounce house for the kids...it's fun for the whole family!

name: Art Hike
description: A 3-mile art hike combining dance, poetry, music, storytelling and environmental art with family, leisure and meditative hikes.

name: New Years Eve Light Parade & Fireworks
description: Let's ring in the New Year in mountain fashion. This free fun event is for intermediate skiers and riders ages 10 or older who can ski or ride unassisted in the dark on the Snowbird run. Come early to secure a spot in the parade. If you are wanting to spectate the event, join us at the bar for a warm drink. The New Year’s Fireworks show will follow the Light Parade.

name: Summer Music Series
description: Every Wednesday from June 20 to August 29, TDRPD hosts a community gathering with great music in the amphitheater located at Truckee Regional Park.

name: Truckee Rodeo
description: The Truckee Professional Rodeo Association strives to enrich our community by keeping the western heritage of our town alive and by giving back to other local organizations each year.

name: Truckee Thursdays
description: Truckee Thursdays is a feel good event that beckons the community to enjoy some exuberant downtown summertime energy. Offering a stage with live music; a food court with an eclectic selection of eats; children’s activities; a beer garden; and a wide variety of artisan vendors.  In addition, attendees will find sidewalk sales and other activities hosted by downtown merchants.

name: Truckee Brewfest
description: Enjoy award-winning craft and homebrews, while listening to live music, and voting for your favorite brew!

name: Truckee Winter Carnival
description: The Winter Carnival has been a Truckee tradition since 1895. Truckee is known as a rustic, historic mountain town, but few people think of it as the town that introduced winter sports to the West.

name: Latitude 39 Festival
description: Latitude 39 is your mountain music experience in Truckee, CA with live music on two stages

name: Truckee Music Festival
description: The festival, in partnership with Truckee Donner Recreation and Park District, kicks off two nights of live music, with an up close and personal music experience against the stunning Truckee RiverView Sports Park backdrop of the Sierra Nevada Mountains.

name: Music in the Park Truckee
description: Music in the Park is a series of free concerts at Truckee River Regional Park. Join us on Wednesdays to see local bands play some great live music all Summer long. It's the perfect Truckee activity, so bring the family, dinner, drinks, and enjoy!

name: The Great Ski Race
description: The Great Ski Race is the only fundraiser for the Tahoe Nordic Search & Rescue Team (TNSAR), a non-profit volunteer organization that provides search and rescue services to the public.

name: Truckee Literary Festival
description:
The Tahoe Literary Festival champions literary arts high in the Sierra Nevada mountains by hosting the Tahoe Literary Festival, the Truckee Literary Crawl and pop-up events throughout the year.  

Inspired by the natural beauty of Truckee and the North Lake Tahoe region our passion is to nurture emerging voices, celebrate established ones and bring community together to listen, share and learn.

name: Truckee Pride Week
description: We love living and playing in the incredible landscape of the Sierra Nevada. Historically, rural towns and outdoor spaces haven't always been accepting of the queer community. We’re working to make sure that regardless of sexual orientation, gender identity, race and lifestyle, everybody feels safe, supported and welcome in the Truckee area.

name: Truckee Roundhouse Annual Makers Show
description:
This event brings together our entire creative community– Makers, Artists, Visionaries, Inventors, Non-profits, Performers, Education Partners, and Sponsors to IGNITE THE IMAGINATION. Discover over 60 local artisans – jewelers, sculptors, and metal workers all together to INSPIRE YOU. This is not just a craft fair – at the Maker Show, YOU are the artist. 

Try sewing, experience wood turning, create your own candle, or explore screen printing. We'll have a makers market, food trucks, beer, music, Robot Rumble, art cars, kids activities, tours of the makerspace, a silent auction, and raffle prizes. Join us for a true celebration of Wonder, Art, and Community.

name: First Saturdays Art Walks
description:
On the first Saturday of every month, downtown Truckee transforms into an open-air celebration of art and creativity. Stroll the charming streets, look for the Art Walk flags, and pop into participating businesses and galleries to meet local artists and explore their work.

It’s an evening of browsing, shopping, sipping, and soaking up the vibrant arts scene that makes Truckee so special. Grab a friend, take a walk, and get inspired—we’ll see you downtown!

name: Moody's Jazz Camp
description: Moody’s Jazz Camp adds an essential dimension to our mountain town by bringing in world class musicians, who, through personalized mentoring, musical role-modeling, sharing the stage, and insisting on some heavy-hitting fun, guide our young people toward an appreciation for jazz and an intimacy with the art form’s inherent qualities: creativity, courage and the expression of individuality through musical ideas.

name: Trails & Vistas Art Hikes
description:
Described as diverse and inspiring, Trails and Vistas Art Hikes blend art experiences with the beauty of being immersed in nature. Specialty Hikes: 9:00 am Mindfulness Hike,  with a Mindful guide you are invited to shift out of the busy-minded analytical thinking and guide your thoughts into mindful sensory immersion in the beauty and wonder of the natural world. The same pace as a regular hike, and a walking in-tune with nature experience. 10:00 am Family Hikes: The hiking paths weave through forests and gorgeous scenery, offering creative experiences around every corner. Enjoy art, dance and music sites set along a 3 mile trail.
11:40 Leisure Hike: Same trail route, but a little more leisure paced hike.

name: Mexican Heritage Festival
description: Fox Cultural Hall hosts the annual Mexican Heritage Festival in honor of Día de La Independencia  The event  starts with a parade into the festival grounds and live music! Activities include live performances; arts n crafts; food, drink and treats, and tons of traditional games including, Loteria, Futbol and many more from your childhood.

name: Annual Truckee River Day & Fair
description: Truckee River Day projects are designed to boost the benefits of large-scale restoration construction that has been completed in recent years. Activities like spreading native seed, planting willows, mulching, building beaver dams, improving trails and stabilizing streambanks help to recharge groundwater, increase moisture to vegetation that supports critical habitat, and keeps water flowing in our streams later into the dry season.

name: Truckee Wine, Walk & Shop
description: The Downtown Truckee Wine, Walk and Shop, which began in 2004, is an annual event that takes place in historic downtown Truckee, California. Since its very first year, the event has become a beloved autumn tradition celebrating the town’s unique history, culture, and community.

name: Truckee's Historical Haunted Tour
description: In October, Trails & Vistas presents an eerie experience exploring spooky ghostly tales in their Truckee’s Haunted Historic Tour. During the walkabout through the area’s sinister past of yesteryear, participants will be guided through the first home, first school, first church, the oldest building and the oldest retail store in Truckee … and, there just may be a haunted visit by one of the ghosts rumored to inhabit these haunts.

name: Tahoe Donner Annual Fall Festival
description: Fall Festival is Truckee’s premier family-friendly autumn celebration and one of Tahoe Donner’s largest community events! Fall Festival is a favorite for all ages, featuring a full day of harvest-themed events and activities.

name: Truckee Tree Lighting Ceremony
description: The evening of the Downtown Holiday Festival is a big night in downtown Truckee, with temperatures dropping and snow falling North Lake Tahoe is ready to welcome the joyous season of winter holidays and a big night of Family-Friendly Fun!

name: Truckee Festive Fridays
description: Enjoy a special Sip ‘n’ Shop, a Snowflake Scavenger Hunt, holiday carolers, visits with Santa, and a festive selfie station to capture the holiday spirit. Stroll through beautifully lit streets, discover unique local shops, and experience the warmth of the season. Bring your family and friends to celebrate in Historic Downtown Truckee!

name: Truckee Certified Farmers Market
description: Bringing you local fresh fruits and veggies, meats, baked goods and artisan crafts. Find us at Truckee Regional Park every Tuesday from 8am-1pm!

name: Art Space
description: Art Space is not only a place for making art but also a therapeutic (art) SPACE that holds room for emotional processing and personal growth through creative expression. As a community-based therapeutic art studio, we are dedicated to supporting children as they navigate developmental milestones, foster meaningful peer connections, and work through personal challenges. At Art Space, we offer a safe and nurturing environment where children can explore their emotions, express themselves creatively, and trust their art process in a supportive, non-judgmental space.

name: Bear River Glassworks
description:
We offer classes and workshops for all skill levels with bench space rental, tools, and raw materials available on site.
Whether you are a curious first-timer or a seasoned professional, we have everything you need to create your vision and stoke your creative fire.

From introductory classes for beginners and foundational courses for those looking to learn the art of glass flameworking, to immersive workshops for those looking to hone a more specific skillset, we offer something for everyone.

We offer bench rental by the day, week, or month with torch, tools, and safety glasses included. 

We also have a wide selection of raw materials, torches, tools, and everything you need to turn your creative dream into a reality. We are a true one-stop shop!

name: C.H.A.M.P. Gallery at City Hall
description: The "CHAMP Gallery City Hall" refers to a youth art exhibition space in Nevada City, California, showcasing young local artists' work (under 25) focusing on community, environment, and culture, with themes changing periodically, like "Youth Visions of Tomorrow" in 2025. It's part of Nevada City's Public Arts initiative, highlighting local talent within City Hall, and artists can submit work for future exhibits.

name: Eric Rood Administration Center Lobby
description:
Public art must always strive to be physically and freely accessible. It can be for simple enjoyment or carry many meanings. It can be set in stone, span a wall, be ephemeral or permanent. It can be fantastic or representational; tell a story, lend civic pride, and remind us that though we may be unique in our identities, we are one.

Our program is about providing a thoughtful space of belonging for all residents of Nevada County, expressing community values, heightening our awareness, and helping us question our assumptions. Art in Public Spaces acknowledges both our California Cultural Districts, and communities that populate our diverse landscapes. It is for everyone, a form of collective community expression, reflecting our society and opening our civic spaces.

name: Holt Ballet Conservatory
description: Holt Ballet Conservatory offers outstanding classical ballet training for youths through adults by following a comprehensive syllabus endorsed by American Ballet Theatre's national curriculum, the Vaganova training method, and the California State Framework for the arts.

name: Indian Springs Art & Ceramic Center
description:
The Indian Springs Art & Ceramic Center is a project art space and gallery devoted to the promotion of the ceramic arts in Nevada County and California. We feature education and experiences in the arts, with survey exhibitions and instruction devoted to the sculptural and functional dialog of ceramic media. 

Our facility offers knowledgeable staff, scheduled juried exhibits, artist workshops and instructional classes with discussion on "making" clay objects and firing technologies used. We provide both private and community artist presentations and events for the public.

ISACC's mission is to foster an appreciation for those interested in this colorful clay story, to create awareness of this unique contemporary survey and provide a wealth of creative and artistic knowledge surrounding the ceramic arts .

name: Neighborhood Center of the Arts
description:
NCA’s roots in Nevada County go back to 1966, when the Community Workshop was founded as a jobs training center for adults with developmental disabilities. Internationally acclaimed ceramicist, Gillian Hodge began an art program at the Community Workshop in 1973. Ten years later, the art program ended due to government cutbacks. In 1984, Hodge and supporters within the community founded the Neighborhood Center of the Arts, a new program to provide arts based training and education. The first location was in a small house in downtown Grass Valley. Over the next ten years, NCA moved several times to accommodate the expanding program.
In 1992 the Nevada County Community Workshop/Community Achievement Center merged with Neighborhood Center of the Arts. NCA introduced the Miguel Quesada Program–our 1:4 Community program–to serve a wider group of people with disabilities. We’ve been located at the Litton Building since 1995. Here, we have been able to expand within this facility as our program grows.

name: The Chambers Project
description: The Chambers Project stands as a beacon of psychedelic art and culture, representing some of the most influential contemporary artists in the field. Founded and curated by Brian Chambers, a passionate advocate for the psychedelic experience, the gallery has become a global hub for artists, enthusiasts, and collectors alike. With its long-standing relationships with renowned figures such as Ralph Steadman, Roger Dean, the Rick Griffin Estate, Oliver Vernon, and Mars 1, The Chambers Project holds an esteemed position as the world’s leading psychedelic art gallery.

name: The Granucci Gallery
description: Donated by art enthusiasts and founding supporters Leo and Gayle Granucci, the Granucci Gallery features an additional 602 square feet of wall space and includes an open floor plan that integrates smoothly with the lobby. The new visual arts gallery will feature works by local artists, as well as special private collections.

name: The Vault @ Crush
description: A boutique showcasing the talents and interests of artist and roller skater Hollie Dilley. Crush is a gallery where I peddle my wares. It’s a clothing boutique. It’s an artistic vision. It’s also a testament to the badassery of the sport of Roller Skating. Welcome to Crush Nevada City, where history meets art in The Vault! In 1856, this space was a bustling Nevada County Bank, and today, we pay homage by transforming it into a captivating gallery. It's a gallery adorned with my taxidermy and a mesmerizing wall showcasing my custom skate designs.

name: ‘UBA SEO: Nisenan Arts and Culture
description: ‘Uba Seo: Nisenan Arts and Culture is a gallery and culture center that brings visibility to the Nevada City Rancheria Nisenan Tribe in their Ancestral Homelands.

name: Wolf Craft School
description: Wolf Craft School, located at The Tinnery in the heart of Nevada City, is a creative hub offering hands-on workshops and classes that reconnect people with the timeless art of making. From hand-built ceramics and fiber arts to fermentation, shoemaking, natural dyeing, and jewelry making, our programs celebrate the beauty of craftsmanship and the satisfaction of creating something from start to finish.

name: Wonder Docent
description: Your accomplice in curiosity. A shop in Nevada City CA with art supplies, stationery, design, objects, books, and workshops.

name: The Parsonage Annex
description: The parsonage 1865 is a timeless blend of Victorian architecture and modern luxury.  Set in the heart of historic Nevada City, the Parsonage 1865 provides a clean canvas and is a fantastic destination to host your event!  We have hosted retreats, concerts, weddings, rehearsals, makers markets, and more!  Contact the Parsonage Directly to inquire about your event.

name: Art Works Gallery Co-op
description: Located in the historic gold mining town of Grass Valley, California, the gallery is at the center of a vibrant visual and performing arts community.  Over 30 juried visual artists cooperate in presenting their work in this 19th century building.  The work includes ceramic, glass, jewelry, painting, woodturning, furniture, fiber art, metal, mosaic, mixed media, photography, and sculpture.

name: Artists Studio in the Foothills (ASIF)
description: The Artists Studio in the Foothills (ASiF) is a 4000 sq. ft., fully-equipped studio art center. In 2008 ASiF opened its doors with a mission to nurture the creative spirit of the community, offer a professional studio atmosphere and creative home for visual artists by providing a place where are can be made, displayed and appreciated.  The center offers classes and workshops in a variety of media for all ages, private and shared studio space for working artists, and a gallery to promote local and visiting artists.

name: BriarPatch Art Gallery and Amphitheater
description: BriarPatch Food Co-op offers art exhibit space for the enjoyment of our customers and to promote local art.

name: Granucci Gallery
description: This aesthetically pleasing display space for the visual arts features shows by local, regional, and international artists. The gallery also serves as an intimate venue for meetings, artist receptions, literary and dramatic readings. The space includes a concessions area and no-host bar.

name: Ellu Gallery
description: Ellu Gallery is a 2,200 sq. ft. artist owned collaborative gallery and event space. Ellu provides custom staging, commercial art lease programs and rents the gallery space for truly unique events and custom shows. Both private and public events are available. Hand selected artists versed in large scale one of a kind paintings and sculptures are featured at Ellu. Life is Art. Ellu Gallery breaks the old paradigm into a timeless lucid dream with an ever expanding collection from Zen surrealism, abstract, Expressionism, blown glass, wood sculpture, to hand made guitars.

name: St. Joseph Cultural Center
description: St. Joseph's Cultural Center is a nonprofit organization dedicated to preserving a historic landmark for cultural activities in the community. It is owned and operated by the Historic Mount St. Mary's Preservation Committee.

name: Lucchesi Vineyards Tasting Room
description: The Lucchesi Tasting Room is open from 11:00 am until the party's over, every day except Easter, Christmas, and Thanksgiving. Our summer hours are extended for special events, please call us for more information.

name: North Star House
description: Our mission is to rehabilitate and transform the Historic North Star House into a premier cultural event center and to promote Nevada County’s legacy by fostering local arts, letters and sciences.

name: North Star Power House & Pelton Wheel Museum
description: The North Star Mining Museum, a part of the Nevada County Historical Society, is one of the gems of Nevada County and exists not only to preserve and protect our area’s mining heritage but also educates and informs people not only about the mining process, but also about the impact the industry had upon the humanity of the people who built the mines and those who toiled there during their operation.

name: Starr Winery Tasting Room
description: Sierra Starr Vineyard and Winery is a small vineyard and winery in the Sierra Foothills AVA. Owned and operated by the Starr Family, Phil and Anne since 1995 and their son Jackson since 2005.

name: Swan Levine House & Printmaking Studio
description: The Swan Levine House plays significantly into the history of Nevada County. The fine art of Printmaking is illustrated by the extensive collection of art throughout the house.

name: The Artist Workshop
description: We offer Pottery, Candle Making, Paint & Sips, Wood Signs, DIY Bags and Pillows and Canvas Art, we also have a great retail section with art supplies. We are a place to come to make gifts, unique pieces for your home or just a place to come and get crafty.

name: The Louvre Framing, Restoration and Gallery
description: Locally owned and operated, we provide top quality custom framing and art restoration. While you're in, visit our on-site gallery.

name: West Wind
description: A Garden Sanctuary Store featuring local artists Deborah Bridges, Jerrianne Van Dyk, Meg Black Smith, Jerry Delong and many others.

name: Wild Eye Pub
description: Wild Eye Pub gives customers great food, inspired drinks, and attentive service in a comfortable, art-filled space; a perfect place to connect with others, nurture creative ideas, and nourish body and spirit.

name: Stucki Jewelers and Engravers
description: Stucki Jewelers specializes in every aspect of fine jewelry. We have a qualified and caring sales staff to help you find the perfect gift for that special loved one or that special something you have always wanted.  We also check and clean your jewelry complimentary, and offer a full service, onsite repair shop for all your repair needs.  By supporting Stucki Jewelers you support us in giving back to our local community, as we continue to donate to more than 45 local organizations

name: Elixart Herbal Lounge & Gallery
description: Elixart is an herbal elixir lounge and visionary art gallery offering the highest integrity and highest vibration ingredients throughout our menu of mood-enhancing elixir drinks, kava, tea, and other exotics; raw food, and premium organic products.

name: Firehouse No.1
description: Completed in the Spring of 1861, this firehouse was home to Nevada Hose Company No.1 from 1861 to 1938. During those nearly 80 years, horse -drawn fire wagons, handcarts and “modern” motorized fire engines rolled out the doors.

name: Grass Roots Antique Tool Museum
description: Our specialty is one-of-a-kind custom installations designed virtually from photos of your home, office or business.

name: Kitkitdizzi
description: It's pronounced: kit-kid-dizzy, but without heavily spacing or over annunciating those four syllables. It's a native plant. Bear Clover, Mountain Misery or Kitkitdizze, as the Miwok referred to it, is endemic to the western side of the Sierra Nevada. If you've hiked in these woods and haven't noticed it underfoot, it's resinous leaves adhering to your pants and shoes, you've most certainly taken in the scent of this highly aromatic little fernlike plant carpeting the forest floor. It is essential to the health of the forests in these parts and for many of us, is the smell of home.

name: LeeAnn Brook Gallery
description: Opened in July 2015, LeeAnn Brook Fine Art is one of the first artist-owned gallery and on site studio in Nevada County, California. Located at 231 Broad St, in the heart of historic downtown Nevada City, the gallery also incorporates the working studio of LeeAnn Brook, where visitors can see paintings in progress and discuss the art with the artist firsthand.

name: Lilly Vigil Gallery
description: Lilly Vigil Gallery is located within the heart of the Sierra Nevada in beautiful downtown Nevada City, California. Since 1985, Vigil's is recognized for unparalleled quality of artistic and cultural treasures, focusing on fine Native American art. The gallery also specializes in originals, graphics, sculptors and exquisite jewelry from local, regional, and national artists.

name: Narrow Gauge Railroad Museum
description: Located in Nevada City, California, the Nevada County Narrow Gauge Railroad and Transportation Museum is dedicated to the preservation of local transportation history and artifacts from the narrow gauge railroad era. Visitors are offered a docent led historical tour of the museum, rail yard, and restoration shop. There is no admission charge.

name: Nevada City Picture Framing
description: Our mission is to provide you, the customer, with an enjoyable full spectrum experience at our shop, whether in custom framing, art restoration, or fine art acquisition. Our friendly staff of experts is always ready to help you create a one of a kind, unique frame design. Stop by our gallery for art and gifts!

name: Searls Historical Library  (NC Historical Society)
description: The Searls Historical Library is the archive of the Nevada County Historical Society.  Established in 1973, the Searls Library has become one of the premier libraries of materials on the area's history, people and mines which contributed to the development of this county.

name: Spirithouse Import Gallery
description: Featuring a fairly traded, personally chosen collection of fine artisan jewelry, clothing and home decor, from the Hearts and Hands of Southeast Asia

name: The Curious Forge
description: Our mission is to provide access to traditional and advanced tools, a large space and a variety of skill sets in a collaborative and innovative environment that enables the members to create what matters to them.

name: Utopian Stone
description: Nestled in the Sierra Nevada Foothills, Utopian Stone was founded in 1974 by G. Patrick Dyer and wife, Diane. We employ four master goldsmiths, plus a fifth, Dean Dussell, who works from his studio in Colorado. At Utopian Stone we specialize in high quality one of a kind work and customer commissions. Our favorite thing is to design for clients who have no idea what they want. As we do not reproduce our one of a kind creations, our customers challenge our designers who may want something similar to a one of a kind piece, but with its own unique personal flair

name: Nevada City Crystal and Glass
description: Swarovski Crystal Living, Swarovski Jewelry, Anna Leece Jewelry, Art Glass, Home Decor, Barware, Custom-made Jewelry, Shopping.

name: Western SkiSport Museum
description: The Western SkiSport Museum was founded in 1969 by the Auburn Ski Club. Working with ski writer and historian William B. Berry, the Museum was developed as an exhibition of Western North American ski history. Today the Museum continues on a mission to collect, preserve and exhibit the history of winter ski sports in the western United States.

name: Art Truckee
description: Art Gallery, Performance and Event Venue and Wine Bar

name: Atelier & Bespoke
description: Founded in 2015, Atelier offers a collection of hand-sourced 2D prints, paintings and designer art wares and an exclusive collection of artist supplies and materials.  Atelier strives to be the creative hub of the local art community by hosting workshops taught by the area’s most sought after artists, illustrators, designers, weavers, knitters, painters and more who share the vision to create always and inspire all.

name: Emigrant Trail Museum - Donner Lake Memorial State Park - Museum
description: The Emigrant Trail Museum contains exhibits about the cultural history of the area, including local Native Americans, the Donner Party, and building of the First Transcontinental Railroad.

name: Gallery 5830’
description: Gallery 5830′ is a creative portal, featuring an eclectic mix of artwork and furniture, hand crafted by artists in the local community and neighboring regions. Through our mission to connect clients with the creative community, Gallery 5830’ offers a selection of in house artwork for sale on consignment. Gallery 5830’ also facilitates artist/ client commissions that emphasize a collaborative vision, site-specific designs and refined old-world craftsmanship.

name: Joseph Research Cabin
description: Truckee Donner Historical Society was founded July 24, 1968 to preserve the history of the Truckee Donner area. Besides maintaining the historic Joseph Research Cabin (the Gateway Cabin), the Society is also responsible for operating the Old Jail Museum which was built in 1875.  The Society maintains the Community Memorial Garden including the Memorial Arch behind the Old Jail Museum, the Donner  Cross, and Schaffer Cemetery.

name: Lorien Powers Studio Jewelry
description: Lorien Powers is an artist who specializes in creating industrial and contemporary style jewelry. Lorien prefers to work with gold, silver, natural gems, and minerals. Known for the decorative features of her work, she employs a wide variety of techniques, including roller printing, cold connecting, riveting, fabricating and forging. She draws inspiration for her jewelry from many different sources, some from nature, some from music (Phish in particular), some from industrial environments.

name: Mountain Arts Collective
description: Located at the Train Depot in historic Downtown Truckee, Mountain Arts Collective began in 2017, with three local artists who wanted to showcase quality art by individuals closely connected to the community. Our goal is to create a welcoming space that will attract locals and visitors with high end statement pieces, more affordable arts & gifts, and everything in between.

name: Mountain Forge
description: Hand forged iron work.

name: Old Jail Museum
description: Truckee’s historic jail museum is one of only a few surviving 19th-century jailhouses of its kind in the West and one of the few remaining original buildings in Truckee. The old Bastille was used continuously from 1875 until May 1964, and ably withstood the many fires that swept the town in its early days.  Although jails are usually sturdy, Truckee’s is a virtual strongbox. The original building consisted of just the lower level, constructed of native stone. The walls are 32-inches thick at the lower level, with no windows unless one counts the small vents for each cell, which are set with irregular rows of two-inch steel bars. The ceilings are plate steel, insulated with dirt, and lined with narrow gauge railroad tracks. All doors are riveted steel, weighing an estimated 200 pounds each.

name: Riverside Studios
description: Riverside Studios mission is to cultivate artistic growth in the Truckee Community while handcrafting one of a kind artistic goods, creating a unique shopping experience for both locals and tourists alike. From paintings to pottery, from leather goods to jewelry and metal sculpture there is something for everyone.

name: Truckee Railroad Museum
description: Truckee Railroad Museum is located next to the historic railroad station in downtown Truckee. This adds historical significance to the area, as the caboose is from the Southern Pacific Railroad and was used in the area. Paint for the caboose matches the original SP paint scheme. Inside the museum are stories, pictures, recreations, and railroad artifacts depicting the impact of railroads in the formation and development of Truckee. The First Transcontinental Railroad, Logging Railroads, and Tourism by Rail all played an important part in Truckee’s history.

name: Truckee Tahoe Airport District (Art at the Airport)
description: Since 1958, the Truckee Tahoe Airport District has been providing a high quality, safe facility for pilots, visitors and the community. Nestled in the Sierra Nevada mountain range at an elevation of 5,900 ft., twelve miles from Lake Tahoe, we promise one of the most scenic flight and visiting experiences in the world. We are proud of our pilot safety and community programs, state of the art energy saving facilities and top notch staff and leadership. We are committed to offering the best small, mountain airport in the Country.

name: Higgins Lions Community Center
description: This non-profit Lion's Community Center is available for use by the general public for events such as meetings, weddings, parties and family gatherings.

name: 9848 Gallery at Truckee Artist Lofts
description:
9848.GALLERY at the Truckee Artist Lofts.  

Original Showcases and Events by Resident Artists.

name: Ridgeline Gallery
description:
Ridgeline Gallery is a contemporary fine art gallery located in the heart of downtown Truckee, California. Specializing in Truckee / Tahoe landscape photography and mountain-inspired paintings, the gallery features bold, sophisticated works that speak to modern mountain living. We proudly represent a curated roster of local, regional, and international mid-career and established artists whose work resonates with discerning collectors, interior designers, and homeowners seeking refined, statement-making pieces.

Exhibitions rotate monthly, with each new show opening during the Truckee Art Walk, held on the first Saturday of every month from 4–7pm. These artist receptions offer an engaging opportunity to meet the artists, enjoy refreshments, and experience new bodies of work in an inspiring, elevated setting.

name: Piper J Gallery
description:
Piper J Gallery is a welcoming art venue located in Truckee, CA showcasing the work by a wide variety of art and artists.

Since it's establishment in 2016, Piper J Gallery has been a place where collectors can discover contemporary art suited for modern mountain living.

Piper J Gallery seeks to inspire collectors while supporting and encouraging local and regional artists in a fresh and inspiring atmosphere. The artwork is a consistently changing, with a new exhibit focusing on feature artists or a themed group show every month. Artist Receptions are held monthly on the first Saturday from 4-7pm during the Truckee Art Walk.

Gallery owner Piper Johnson and team provide comprehensive services in curation, installation, art consulting and facilitating custom commissions.

name: La Galleria
description: La Galleria has been a staple in the local community for over 38 years. We provide treasures from around the world featuring one of a kind gifts for any occasion, any age group, of all prices. In store and soon online you can find jewelry, fashionable clothing and home décor of all kinds.

name: Steve Randall Community Recreation Center
description: The Steve Randall Community Recreation Center is a multi-use facility complete with a gymnasium, fitness center, climbing wall, dance room, meeting rooms, kitchen, and more.

name: Town of Truckee Exhibition Hall
description: Truckee, CA is nestled in the Sierra Nevadas. We provide land use, public safety, and public works services to our community. Welcome to Truckee!

name: LIFT Workspace
description: We built Lift to help people work better, together. Remote work gave us freedom — but also isolation. Local professionals need connection, flexibility, and a sense of belonging. Nonprofits and entrepreneurs need affordable room to grow. Truckee needs a space where ambition and community can thrive side by side—while empowering sustainable growth for people and place alike.

name: UBA SEO: Nisenan Arts and Culture
Indian Springs Art and Ceramic Center
Nevada County Narrow Gauge Railroad Museum
Center for the Arts
description: The Center for the Arts is a cultural and educational organization that promotes and presents the literary, visual, and performing arts for the enrichment of our community.

name: Del Oro Theater
description: Sierra Theaters is a locally owned, family business that operates Sierra Cinemas, The Del Oro Theatre, Sutton Cinemas, and The Nevada Theatre Film Series. Each theatre features comfortable seats, friendly staff, and state-of-the-art projection and sound. The popcorn is delicious, too!

name: Don Baggett Theater
description:
Have an Interesting presentation coming up? How about an important conference?
The Don Baggett Theatre Is the place for you. Our facilities Provide with access to a theatre, our own in house technicians and use of our scene shop/green room.

name: Grass Valley Veterans Memorial Hall
description: This treasured historic venue is the meeting and special events facility for veterans groups including the American Legion Post No.130, Disabled American Veterans, Marine Corps League, and the Vietnam Veterans. As a service to the community this facility is available to rent year around. This multipurpose venue was renovated in 2008 to provide modern comfort while preserving its historic character.

name: Lucchesi Vineyards & Winery
description: We specialize in traditionally crafted wines produced in small lots to capture their varietal character and regional qualities. We are currently growing 16 different varietals at our winery in Grass Valley including Zinfandel, Merlot, Chardonnay, Sauvignon Blanc Muscat, Sauvignon Blanc, Pinot Grigio, Syrah, Cabernet Franc, Viognier, Cabernet Sauvignon, Tempranillo, Petite Sirah, and Petit Verdot, and three Portuguese Port Varietals: Tinto Cão, Tinta Negra Molé, and Touriga Nacional. We hope you come out for wine tasting at the Vineyard.

name: Nevada County Fairgrounds
description: Located in Grass Valley, California, the Nevada County Fairgrounds has been declared as  California's Most Beautiful Fairgrounds, and is covered with hundreds of towering pine trees on almost 90 acres. It is home to the annual Nevada County Fair, the Draft Horse Classic, the Country Christmas Faire, world-class music festivals, and year-round community events. There is also a year-round RV Park, Lions Lake, rental facilities, weekly events, weddings, some of our community's largest outdoor events, and lots of areas to walk.

name: North Star House
description: The North Star House is a 10,000 square foot mansion, built in the California Craftsman style in 1905.  The North Star House is listed on the National and State Registers of Historic Places. It is located on a portion of the former North Star Mine, a 14-acre site of spectacular Gold Country landscapes. The North Star House is owned, operated and being lovingly restored by the North Star Historic Conservancy, a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization.

name: Off Center Stage
description: Our professionally equipped studio theater located at the back of our building at 315 Richardson Street offers an intimate space suitable for a wide variety of performances and events.

name: Prime Cinemas Sutton
description: Sierra Theaters is a locally owned, family business that operates Sierra Cinemas, The Del Oro Theatre, Sutton Cinemas, and The Nevada Theatre Film Series. Each theatre features comfortable seats, friendly staff, and state-of-the-art projection and sound.æ The popcorn is delicious, too!

name: Miners Foundry Cultural Center
description: The Miners Foundry Cultural Center is a living historic treasure serving Nevada County as a cultural arts and community events center.  More than a venue, the nonprofit's mission is to preserve, enhance and utilize the historic Miners Foundry for cultural, educational and social activities in Nevada County.

name: Nevada Theatre
description: The Nevada Theatre is the oldest existing theatre building on the west coast that continues to operate as a theatre. We are now celebrating 152 years of history, live performances, music, movies and art in Nevada County.

name: North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center
description: The North Columbia Schoolhouse Cultural Center gives the rural San Juan Ridge community a local place to meet, to share its creativity, and to participate in a wide variety of enriching activities. Located 20 miles north of Nevada City, the Schoolhouse was built by the mining community in 1875, and has a long tradition of serving the community as a school and public meeting hall.

name: Off Broadstreet Theatre
description: We produce only comedies, and most of these are musicals, with a slant toward familiar hit songs of the 1950's and 60's. Intimate cabaret seating and a relaxed and friendly atmosphere add to the uniqueness of the experience.  Also, we provide a modestly priced selection of refreshments, including locally produced wines, desserts, coffee and other beverages, served at your table before the show and during intermission.

name: Pioneer Park Bandshell
description: The Bandshell is designed for acoustic music and is therefore a nice location for an event offered by a concert band or small music event. Amplified music is discouraged at this location. The Bandshell is also a nice location for a family & friends gathering. Power, water and private bathrooms are available at this location.

name: The Onyx Theater
description: The Onyx Theatre is dedicated to bringing the very best in independent, foreign language, and documentary film to our region. We make a conscious commitment to screen works from underrepresented communities and alternative perspectives; fostering understanding and the creative spirit.

name: Shady Creek Events Center
description: Shady Creek Outdoor School and Conference Center is a 132-acre property located in the Sierra Nevada Foothills just outside of Nevada City in California's Gold Country.

name: Art Truckee
description: Art Gallery, Performance and Event Venue, Wine Bar

name: Community Arts Center
description: Our auditorium at the CAC is well-equipped for your next public or private event. Features include a large stage, theatrical lighting, and live audio production equipment. You can also project films or other media content to a full-size cinema screen, the only one in Truckee!

name: McIver Arena
description: The McIver Rodeo Arena hosts the annual Truckee Rodeo featuring the Truckee Donner Horsemen. It is also available to rent for a variety of horse-friendly uses.

name: Salty Gebhardt Amphitheater -Truckee River Park
description: Set near the Truckee River, just across the Legacy Trail, lies The Salty Gebhart Amphitheater. An outdoor entertainment venue with comfortable lawn seating overlooks a generous stage for all sorts of performances. It's also the home of our annual Wednesday Summer Music Series! Popular uses include weddings, concerts, and plays.

name: The Village Cinemas
description: This movie theater at a ski resort shows Hollywood features on 2 screens & has stadium seating.

name: Word after Word Books
description: Word After Word Books is a welcoming space for the community and visitors to connect around books. We carry titles by local authors, regional and history books about the area, and books and magazines pertaining to the outdoor lifestyle in our community. We also have the latest bestsellers and favorite staff picks. There is a section for children and young adults, the Kids & Teens Corner, with comfy beanbag chairs. The Word After Word Team hosts author events, writing workshops, book clubs, and story times.

name: Tour of Nevada City Bicycle Shop
Bear Yuba Land Trust
description: Bear Yuba Land Trust is a private, non profit, membership supported organization that works to promote voluntary conservation of the region’s natural, historical and agricultural legacy. The Land Trust provides a lasting community heritage by protecting and enhancing meadows, watersheds, forests, farms and ranches, trails and parks.  BYLT exists to create a balance between nature and the needs of people like you who make a life and a livelihood here.  Our mission is to enrich your deep community connection with the land  today, tomorrow, and forever.

name: City of Grass Valley Historical Commission
description: It is the intent of the City to encourage the preservation of the City's historical and architecturally significant resources by the formation of a Historical Commission which has the duty and function to identify and designate historical properties within the City and to honor those property owners who have maintained or restored these historical properties.

name: Daughters of the American Revolution – Capt. John Oldham Chapter
description: The DAR, founded in 1890 and headquartered in Washington, DC is a non profit, non-political, volunteer women's service organization dedicated to promoting patriotism, preserving American history, and securing America's future through better education for children.

name: Historic Mount St. Mary’s Preservation Committee
description: St. Joseph's Cultural Center is a nonprofit organization dedicated to preserving a historic landmark for cultural activities in the community. It is owned and operated by the Historic Mount. St. Mary's Preservation committee.

name: Nevada County Gem & Mineral Society
description: The Nevada County Gem and Mineral Society is a non-profit organization that has the following objectives:  To promote the study of mineralogy, geology, and fossils.  To encourage the collection of minerals and gems. To foster the study and practice of the lapidary arts.  To provide field trips to mineral localities.  To promote good fellowship, education and recreation.

name: North Star Historic Conservancy
description: The Conservancy is working to re establish the unique, historic North Star House and grounds into a premier cultural event center. It is committed to maintaining the property for the public by promoting tours, events educational activities and the arts.

name: Gold Country Celtic Society
description: The Gold Country Celtic Society was organized to celebrate the culture of these proud people, many of whom, especially the Cornish and Welsh, worked the hard rock mines and made our Nevada City Grass Valley area famous. The Society wishes to perpetuate Celtic traditions of music, dance,language, and costume.

name: Nevada Cemetery District
description: The Nevada Cemetery District is an independent special district charged with providing respectful and cost effective interment services appropriate for the diversity of local conditions and circumstances. It is responsible for the improvement, expansion and operation of public cemeteries in Western Nevada County, including historic cemeteries established by fraternal, pioneer, religious, social and other organizations

name: Nevada City Rancheria of the Nisenan Tribe
description: The Nevada City Rancheria was created from an executive order by President Woodrow Wilson in 1913 from a land allotment obtained by Chief Charlie Cully in 1887. Cully's land on Cement Hill became Federal Trust Land and the Tribal government became a federally recognized entity. The Nevada City Rancheria was one of 48 Rancherias illegally terminated in the 1950s and 1960s by the California Rancheria Termination Acts. Most have been restored; the Nevada City Nisenan Rancheria has not. Yet.

name: Nevada County Cultural Preservation Trust
description: The Miners Foundry Cultural Center is a living historic treasure serving Nevada County as a cultural arts and community events center. More than a venue, the nonprofit’s mission is to preserve, enhance and utilize the historic Miners Foundry for cultural, educational and social activities in Nevada County. It is a symbol of California’s rich historical heritage, providing an educational and social bridge from our past to the present and future.

name: Nevada County Historical Society
description: The Nevada County Historical Society had its beginning in the fall of 1944 after being inspired by Dr. Robert E. Burns, President of the College of the Pacific. The objective of the new organization was to preserve every source of Nevada County history. Monthly programs were planned to entertain and educate members, and the committee chairmen and groups formed the foundation of the society by collecting, displaying, and preserving the memorabilia of the county.

name: Nevada Theatre Commission
description: The Nevada Theatre Commission is a group of volunteers who are dedicated to making sure the Nevada Theatre continues to be available and usable to our community for years to come.  The Nevada Theatre is owned and maintained by the the Commission which is a non profit 501(c)(3) organization.

name: Ngakmang Foundation
description: To preserve the spiritual, medical, and cultural traditions of the Himalayan and Tibetan region.

name: South Yuba River Citizens League
description: SYRCL unites the community to protect and restore the Yuba River. Motivated by our love for this watershed, we advocate powerfully, engage in active stewardship, educate the public, and inspire activism from the Sierra to the sea.

name: The Sierra Fund
description: The Sierra Fund’s mission is to restore ecosystem and community resiliency in the Sierra Nevada.

name: California Heritage Indigenous Research Project
description: CALIFORNIA HERITAGE: INDIGENOUS RESEARCH PROJECT (CHIRP) was created to research, document, preserve, and protect California Indigenous Nisenan culture. CHIRP continues to support the Nisenan in their quest to reestablish themselves as the Indigenous people in the foothills where their families have resided for thousands of years. CHIRP's area of influence is mainly the counties of Sierra, Yuba, Sutter, Nevada, Placer and Butte.

name: Famous Marching Presidents Inc.
description: The Famous Marching Presidents organization honors the U.S. Constitution in the involvement of the Nevada City Constitution Day Parade and other related public celebrations.

name: Donner Summit Historical Society
description: Donner Summit Historical Society is dedicated to telling the story of the most important historical square mile in California and maybe the entire Western U.S. We operate a museum on the Summit as well as the 20 Mile Museum along Old Highway 40.

name: C A T T Community Project
description: CATT Community Project is a 501c(3) non profit, charitable organization established in October 1999 as a sponsored arm of the Contractors Association of Truckee Tahoe. CATT Community Project is governed by a 10 member Board of Directors which meets monthly. CATT Community Project provides design and construction assistance to other non profits and governmental agencies as a way of giving back to the community.

name: Mountain Area Preservation Foundation
description: To preserve the Truckee Tahoe region’s mountain character and natural environment for present and future generations.

name: Truckee Donner Historical Society
description: Truckee Donner Historical Society was founded July 24, 1968 to preserve the history of the Truckee Donner area. Besides maintaining the historic Joseph Research Cabin (the Gateway Cabin), the Society is also responsible for operating the Old Jail Museum which was built in 1875.  The Society maintains the Community Memorial Garden including the Memorial Arch behind the Old Jail Museum, the Donner  Cross, and Schaffer Cemetery.

name: Truckee Donner Horsemen
description: Truckee Donner Horsemen is a non-profit organization of people who love horses. We are based in Truckee, CA in the high Sierra mountains. Our Mission is to Promote Horsemanship and Preserve the  McIver Arena

name: Truckee Donner Railroad Society
description: The mission of the Truckee Donner Railroad Society is to perpetuate the study and advancement of knowledge of working railroads in the Truckee Donner area with the goal of building a railroad museum to preserve and share what we have learned.

name: Old Truckee Jail Museum
description:
As volunteers for this non profit 501(c)(3) organization, we are stewards of a number of unique physical assets (see below). The TDHS invites you to join with us in the preservation and maintenance of these irreplaceable historical venues, and at the same time have lots of fun in the process discovering our Truckee, California heritage.
Our MISSION is to discover, procure and preserve whatever may relate to the aboriginal, natural, civil, literary, and ecclesiastical history of the Town of Truckee and surrounding area.  Our VISION is to preserve and protect the history of Truckee.
We are a small group of very dedicated volunteers who love Truckee and its rich history, and who continue to serve in whatever capacities are required to continue our mission.

name: Museum of Truckee History
description: The Museum of Truckee History is a non-profit collaboration between the Truckee-Donner Historical Society and the Truckee Donner Railroad Society. The museum is staffed and supported entirely by volunteers from the community. The mission of the museum is to bring Truckee’s history alive by illustrating the stories of its major industries through the display of artifacts and exhibits, creation of documentary films, and access to lectures, educational programs, and research facilities.

name: Truckee Donner Land Trust
description: Since 1990, the Land Trust has not only preserved more than 40,000 acres of open space, but has also built trails, manages a campground, and has created numerous opportunities for the public to access and enjoy these lands.

name: The Martis Fund
description: The Martis Fund was created in 2006 by conservationists and landowners committed to the long-term future of Martis Valley. The Fund supports programs to conserve open space; manage and restore habitat and forest lands; and promote opportunities for workforce housing and related community purposes in the Martis Valley Region. We fulfill our mission primarily through grants to qualifying 501 (C) (3) non-profit organizations active in the region. Funding for our grant programs comes from private community benefit fees (transfer fees). The Fund’s primary role is managing and dispersing financial resources raised in connection with real estate sales (transfer fees) at Martis Camp. We connect non-profit organizations to financial resources to complete projects serving our mission.

name: Truckee River Watershed Council
description: We believe a more sustainable tomorrow starts with the watershed. Our goal is to restore the resiliency of our environment—so nature and humanity can thrive together for generations.

name: Donner Memorial State Park Visitor Center
description: Welcome to the Visitor Center ... Open 7 days a week, 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.. The museum exhibits and gift shop are open daily during the winter season.

description: Made and installed by Curious Forge. Description: Custom built bike rack in the shape of 5 salmon arching out of the pavement. Evolution:  Amy and Chamba Cooke (owners's Summer Thyme's) approached Liam Ellerby at the Curious Forge with the idea of a bike rack as an art piece. The piece intends to raise awareness of the salmon in our watershed, foster bicycle use as green transportation, and encourage the enjoyment of art as practical and useful.

name: Fountain sculpture at Pioneer Park
description: Design: Francis Lloyd, Sculptor: Norman Grag. The fountain was installed in 1976 with funds from the John J. Looser estate. The fountain was designed by architect Francis Lloyd and the metal sculpture was created by artist Norman Grag. The fountain was placed in the middle of an existing city street that ran through the park. The placement of the fountain resulted in an intentional redirection of the road.

name: Mural at Pioneer Park swimming pool
description: Artist: Jordan Mal did the project in 2016 as her Senior Project. Mural on the wall at the swimming pool -- serves as a painting and safety signage.  The wave on the wall matches the depth of the swimming pool, so if a child stands by the wall and the wave is over their head, they know that if they walk straight from that spot into the poolду_the water will be over their head.

name: Robinson Plaza -- Mining sculptures: Pelton Water Wheel and Five Stamp Mill
description: Pelton Water Wheel: Gift from PGE and Nevada City, dedicated Sept 17 1987. Pelton Water Wheel was in continuous use at PGE Drum Power House No. 4 from 1928-1987. 12 ft diameter, 15 tons.  Five-Stamp Mill: (1893) used to crush gold ore at Fortuna Mine near Nevada City. Donated to Nevada City by Bill Magers. Dedicated July 4, 1982

name: Robinson Plaza -- Metal sculpture
description: Artist:  Miriam Morris. Sculpture designed to block electrical boxes.

name: Calanan Park -- Metal sculpture
description: Artist:  Miriam Morris. Like Robinson Plaza, a metal sculpture designed to block electrical box.

description: Shaft Drill Core Monitor

name: Chinese Monument
description: The  Chinese Monument consists of a large rock, babbling fountain and dual plaques in English and old Chinese characters, all surrounded by natural landscaping.

description: Artist:   David Randolf Griffis.  David says ""I painted the ma trux mural 16 years ago for a roast beef sandwich.""  Mother Trucker's is like ""the town square of the ridge.""

Located in front of the Community Center facing the 49 @29190 Hwy 49, North San Juan

description: Artist: Grace Mack, Gem Visuals. Completed September 2016

description: Artist: Grace Mack, Gem Visuals. First two sides completed August 2017. Third wall completed May 2018

name: Life Cycle of the Monarch"" mural project
description: Concept: Avani Patrick. Four images designed by Grace Mack Gem Visuals, then transferred (with help from Stephanie Hubbard and Avani Patrick) to prepared plywood. The whole community was invited to come together to paint it.

name: The Community Center Sign
description: Artist: Grace Mack, Gem Visuals. Hand painted, two sided, 40 sq ft. Both faces of the sign (about 20 sq ft each) were individually hand painted over the spring and summer, then set into this frame by Avani Patrick, Nikko and team.

name: Iron sculpture -- Ridge Cafн© sign
description: Artist: Eric Clausen.  An iron sculpture/sign at The Ridge Cafe. A cafe frequented by artists, farmers, craftsmen, tradesmen, mountain folk, neighbors, travelers, out-door enthusiasts, health-nuts, hippies & hillbillies.

name: Paintings at Penn Valley Chamber of Commerce
description: Three paintings by local artist Curre hang at the Chamber.

name: DeRailed""  bike rack
description: Artist: Bernie LaForest of Striker Forge.  ''DeRailed'' which is part of the Truckee Artistic Bike Rack Project by TPAC, is made of repurposed rail steel. Created by artist Bernie LaForest of Striker Forge, it represents the railRd. and its role bringing commerce to California. It is fittingly placed in the plaza across from Truckee Train Station and was commissioned by the Town of Truckee.

name: High Iron""  bike rack.
description: Artist: Bernie LaForest, Striker Forge. ''High Iron'' by Bernie LaForest of Striker Forge is the most vertical in the Truckee Artistic Bike Rack project. The sculpture is made of rail steel and was commissioned by the Town of Truckee.

name: Locomotion"" bike sculpture
description: Artist: Fred Besch. ''Locomotion'' was created by local kinetic artist Fred Besch. The giant bike sculpture can be found in the plaza west of the historic Flying A building. The sculpture represents residents' love of biking. The art shares plaza space with a giant chess board that was created and donated by Truckee Roundhouse Makerspace.

name: Martis Sculpture"" - front of Martis Outlook professional building.
description: Artist: Anton Standteiner, Mountain Forge. ''Martis Sculpture'' was created by artist Anton Standteiner of The Mountain Forge and commissioned by the Town of Truckee.The flow and style of the sculpture honors the Native American people who once inhabited Martis Valley. It stands in front of the Martis Outlook professional building on the corner of Soaring Way and Truckee Tahoe Airport Way.

name: Mountain Flowers"" sculpture
description: Artist: Troy Corliss, Gallery 5830'. Mountain Flowers sculpture stands 16-18 feet high in the roundabout across from the Truckee Donner Community Center on Donner Pass Rd..  The sculpture was a gift to the Town of Truckee from artist Troy Corliss of Gallery 5830'. Other contributors, in services or supplies, were The Mountain Forge, JMC Industrial Finishing, Huldrege & Kull, and Perennial Landscape & Nursery.

name: The Band""
description: Designed by Anton Standteiner, this metal sculpture suggests the flow and movement of music.

name: The Feather""  bike rack project.
description: Artist: Lauren Bello Okerman.  ''The Feather'' is part of the Truckee Artistic Bike Rack Project by TPAC. It was designed by Lauren Bello Okerman and is made of powder coated steel. The art serves as a bike rack in front of the Truckee Donner Recreation & Park District. This sculpture was sponsored by the Queen of Hearts Women's Fund.

name: Animal Tracks"" -- bike rack project. At Tahoe Forest Hospital.
description: Artist: Kevin Kremler.  ''Animal Tracks'' is a painted steel bike rack sculpture featuring paw prints of local animals. It was made by artist Kevin Kremler and is part of the TPAC Truckee Artistic Bike Rack Project. It was commissioned by Jae Gustafson for Tahoe Forest Hospital.

name: Community As Ecosystem""
description: Artists: Sara L. Smith, Toni Standteiner/Mountain Forge.  Commissioned by the TRDRPD, 'думCommunity As Ecosystem" main mural image measures more than 11 ft. high by 16 ft. wide.  The piece celebrates Truckee's new Aquatic Center donors. The artists represent the concept of joyful balance noting each donor (no matter level of contribution) is a vital part of the overall effort.  The mural speaks to the wider concept of community as an ecosystem, and reminds us that we are all essential. The artists note: "The mural is painted using sepia tones to tie us back to our past, and is painted upon a wall created with reclaimed Lake Tahoe pier wood, creating a tangible tie to our physical past and present.  It depicts a stylized image of Martis Valleyду»s diverse natural environment, and donors are represented by a combination of cut and manipulated animal shapes and forged iron environmental elements, such as mountain peaks, cattails and wave forms in the creek.  On a separate panel each and every donorду»s name has been hand lettered, grouped next to its corresponding donor category and animal shape.дуќ In addition two additional small panels were created to specifically recognize the individuals who helped shepherd the project to fruition, and well as the organizations that contributed multi-million dollar grant funds.

name: Aspen Grove"" in Radiation Lobby at Tahoe Forest Cancer Center
description: Artist: Troy Corliss.  The colorful array of glass leaves in the Troy Corliss думAspen Groveдуќ welcomes patients and visitors to the Radiation Therapy waiting space. Fabricated in steel and kiln fused glass, the artwork integrates an experience of the natural world with interior architecture. The gentle curves of the tree trunks and the subtle patterning and warm colors of the glass leaves creates a three dimensional design that is both intriguing and calming. The Grove can be experienced as a powerful whole at first sight or explored with interest with repeated visits.
